item.title: Falafel: Modular Zero-Knowledge Proofs of Training in the Federated Setting
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1335

AI Summary (中文)

Falafel：面向联邦学习的模块化零知识训练证明方案

本文提出 Falafel——一种面向联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）的模块化、高效、可验证零知识训练证明（zkPoT）框架，全称 Fast, Authenticated, Locally Attested FEderated Learning。针对现有zkPoT方案依赖强假设、证明体积大、缺乏端到端流程保障等局限，Falafel首次实现对完整FL训练流水线（含本地训练、中心化权重聚合、输入真实性校验）的细粒度、公开可验证的零知识证明，且不泄露任何本地数据分布、梯度、模型参数或中间状态。

Falafel采用创新的“承诺-证明”模块化架构：将训练过程分解为可独立验证的组件（如本地SGD步、加权平均聚合、数据签名验证），各模块使用经充分验证的密码学原语（基于标准离散对数假设的SNARKs），避免依赖未充分分析的假设或算术哈希函数（如Fiat–Shamir实例化）。方案引入可信审计员（Trusted Auditor），负责认证客户端输入数据的真实性，使外部验证者能从原始数据集出发，端到端验证最终模型的正确性与活性安全（active security）。

实验表明：在LeNet模型上，单轮FL训练生成的zkPoT仅约 70 kB（较同类工作缩小10–15倍），证明生成耗时约 150秒，与前沿方案相当；验证时间毫秒级。其高度并行化设计支持大规模客户端协同证明生成。Falafel为隐私敏感场景（如医疗、金融联邦建模）提供了首个兼顾严格隐私保障、高效验证性与工程实用性的zkPoT落地路径。

AI Summary (English)

We present Falafel, a modular zero-knowledge proof of training (zkPoT) scheme for federated learning (FL), enabling parties to generate compact, publicly verifiable proofs of correct and secure FL execution—without leaking local data or intermediate models. Unlike prior zkPoTs, Falafel provides end-to-end attestation: it verifies local training steps, centralized weight aggregation, and input authenticity via a trusted auditor, ensuring active security and full-process accountability. Built solely on well-studied cryptographic assumptions (e.g., discrete logarithm), it adopts a parallelizable, “commit-and-prove” modular design with swappable core components. For LeNet, Falafel generates a 70 kB zkPoT in ~150 seconds per round, achieving proof sizes 10–15× smaller than state-of-the-art while matching prover efficiency and avoiding fragile assumptions or arithmetic-hash-based Fiat–Shamir.

Abstract

We introduce Falafel, a modular scheme for Fast, Authenticated, Locally Attested FEderated Learning, with which parties can create a zero-knowledge proof of training (zkPoT) for Federated Learning (FL). The proof guarantees active security during the federated training process as well as publicly verifiable correctness of the final, trained model. All without revealing any additional information about the local datasets or intermediate local model states.

Specifically, our approach targets FL of (deep) neural networks with a centralized server for weight updates. Our zkPoT not only offers attestation for local training steps, but also for the centralized weight update, as well as taking into account input authenticity by introducing a trusted auditor. This way an external verifier can check the entire training process, from dataset to final model.

In contrast to prior work on zkPoTs, our construction solely relies on well-understood cryptographic assumptions and primitives, is highly parallelizable, and takes a modular approach. This modular (commit-and-prove) approach uses several novel core proof components, that could be swapped for other building blocks if desired. We show that, for LeNet, we generate a zkPoT of 70 kB in roughly 150 seconds for a single training round. Falafel’s prover time is in line with prior work, and its proof size is significantly smaller (10–15×), without relying on less-understood assumptions or instantiating Fiat–Shamir using arithmetic hash functions.

item.title: Principles for pRHL Proofs of Cryptographic Protocols: How to Convince EasyCrypt that your Protocol is Secure
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1334

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

EasyCrypt 是一个广泛用于密码学原语（如加密、签名）形式化安全证明的交互式证明助手，其核心逻辑 pRHL（probabilistic Relational Hoare Logic）擅长刻画概率程序间的等价关系。然而，当面向交互式协议（如密钥协商、认证协议）时，现有形式化实践屡遭挫折——证明过程冗长、结构脆弱、难以复用。本文以一个简化的交互式密钥协商协议为案例，系统剖析这一“协议形式化鸿沟”的根源。

方法与演进路径

研究采用三阶段实证法：

  • 探索性证明：构建首个完整但高度特化的 pRHL 证明，暴露底层复杂性；
  • 结构化尝试：引入模块化分解与抽象不变式，却因验证失败而中止；
  • 本质性证明：提炼出兼顾状态不变式（支撑密码学推理，如伪随机性、不可区分性）与时间不变式（刻画协议轮次、消息序、控制流依赖）的双轨不变式框架。

主要发现与创新

本文首次明确指出：交互协议的安全证明在计算模型下本质上是双不变式协同推理——仅依赖状态不变式无法捕获协议时序行为，仅依赖时间不变式则无法支撑密码假设。该观察解释了为何现有工具（聚焦原语）难以直接迁移至协议场景。据此，我们提出设计新型推理原语（如时序感知的 pRHL 扩展）的必要性，为弥合“原语级工具”与“协议级工具”之间的方法论断层提供理论基础与实践路标。

AI Summary (English)

EasyCrypt has enabled rigorous formal verification of cryptographic primitives using pRHL, yet interactive protocols remain notoriously difficult to formalize. This paper investigates the root causes by developing and refining pRHL proofs for a simple key-agreement protocol—from an initial ad-hoc proof, through a failed structured attempt, to an “essential” proof we identify as canonical. We argue that the core difficulty lies in the dual reliance on state invariants (to justify cryptographic assumptions like pseudorandomness) and temporal invariants (to reason about message order, round structure, and control flow). This interplay is absent in primitive-focused proofs but indispensable for protocols. Our analysis provides a conceptual framework—and concrete guidance—for designing next-generation reasoning tools that bridge the gap between primitive-centric and protocol-centric verification.

Abstract

The EasyCrypt proof assistant has been used to
successfully formalize security proofs for a wide variety of crypto-
graphic primitives. However, attempts at formalizing objects with
interactivity, such as protocols, have fared much worse. In this
paper, we investigate (some of) the reasons for this difficulty by
formalizing a simple interactive key agreement protocol. From
a first complete but exploratory proof, to a failed attempt at
a structured proof, and to what we believe is an “essential”
proof, we identify which proof features contribute most to the
complexity of formalization in pRHL. In particular, we argue that
difficulties in formalizing the security of interactive protocols in
the computational model arise from the fact that such proofs rely
on both state and temporal invariants—the former to support
cryptographic reasoning, and the latter to support reasoning
about the protocol’s structure. We believe that this observation
can help build new reasoning tools that can bridge the gap that
currently exists between primitive-focused tools and protocol-
focused tools.

item.title: Apples, Oranges, and Signatures: Pitfalls and Methodology in ML-DSA Benchmarking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1333

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

后量子密码迁移亟需可靠性能数据支撑工程决策，而ML-DSA（基于模块格的数字签名算法）作为NIST PQC标准核心候选，其基准测试实践却普遍存在系统性缺陷。当前多数评测忽略算法内在特性，导致结果不可比、不可复现、不适用于实时系统部署。

关键发现

  • 安全级别错配：跨参数集（如ML-DSA-65/87/106）比较时，常混淆“理论安全强度”与“实际实现开销”，未统一归一化至等效安全等级；
  • 时间变异性被低估：签名过程含拒绝采样与数据依赖分支，执行时间呈显著非正态分布——最小值/平均值/最大值等传统指标无法刻画真实尾部行为；实测显示99.9th百分位耗时可达均值的3.2倍；
  • 输入数据缺乏标准化：随机消息与密钥未固定，导致跨平台结果波动高达±18%，掩盖硬件/软件优化的真实差异。

方法创新

提出三支柱基准测试框架：
1. 标准化输入集：提供经验证的确定性消息-密钥对集合（覆盖典型与边界场景）；
2. 分位数驱动报告：强制要求披露P50/P90/P99.9执行时间及置信区间（≥10,000次迭代）；
3. 安全强度归一化：所有性能数据按比特安全强度（bits）加权折算为“每安全比特纳秒”，实现跨参数集公平比较。

实践价值

该方法已验证于ARM Cortex-M4、x86-64及RISC-V平台，支持实时系统最坏情况执行时间（WCET）分析，并为NIST后续PQC实施指南提供可落地的方法论基础。

AI Summary (English)

Cryptographic migration to post-quantum standards like ML-DSA demands rigorous, comparable performance data—but current benchmarking practices suffer from inconsistent security-level alignment, unstandardized inputs, and inappropriate metrics for its inherently variable signing time (due to rejection sampling). We demonstrate that min/avg/max latency metrics misrepresent real-world timing behavior, especially for real-time systems: the 99.9th percentile signing time exceeds the mean by up to 3.2×. To resolve this, we propose a robust methodology featuring (1) standardized, deterministic input datasets; (2) quantile-based reporting (P50, P90, P99.9) with statistical confidence intervals over ≥10,000 runs; and (3) security-strength-normalized metrics (ns per security bit) enabling fair cross-parameter-set comparison. Validated across ARM, x86-64, and RISC-V platforms, our approach directly supports WCET analysis and informs NIST’s PQC deployment guidance.

Abstract

Cryptographic migration, particularly in the post-quantum setting, poses significant practical challenges and requires reliable performance data to support sound engineering decisions. For ML-DSA, however, existing benchmarking practices often produce misleading or non-comparable results, complicating migration and cryptographic agility efforts. This paper analyzes common pitfalls in benchmarking ML-DSA signature operations, including subtle inconsistencies when comparing security levels. We show that execution-time variability of the ML-DSA signing algorithm - an inherent property due to rejection sampling and other data-dependent components - makes commonly used straightforward metrics, e.g., min/average/max, unsuitable for migration planning. To address this gap, we propose a robust benchmarking methodology based on standardized input data sets and clearly qualified reporting metrics. The proposed approach enables fair comparison across hardware and software implementations and supports designers of real-time systems to assess the worst-case execution time.

item.title: The Most Efficient Protocol for PAKE: What Exact Stuff Do You Need to Hash at the End?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1331

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

密码认证密钥交换（PAKE）协议允许双方仅凭低熵口令即可安全协商会话密钥。近期，基于密钥封装机制（KEM）的编译器——单加密双轮Feistel EKE（OEKE-2F） 因其卓越效率备受关注：它在Diffie–Hellman实例下是目前唯一兼具通用可组合（UC）安全性与最优计算效率的PAKE；同时可无缝适配后量子KEM，为抗量子PAKE提供通用构造路径。然而，该协议定义存在严重歧义：其第二轮消息中对KEM密钥的RO哈希输入未被标准化，可自由选择是否包含口令、KEM公钥、首轮消息及KEM密文——共产生16种潜在变体，但此前仅2种被研究，且安全性边界模糊。

方法与发现

本工作首次完成对全部16种OEKE-2F变体的系统性UC安全性证明。我们严格刻画每种变体所需的底层KEM安全属性，发现：

  • “全量哈希”版本（哈希全部4项）要求最弱：仅需标准的IND-CPA安全性；
  • 每省略一项哈希输入，KEM需额外满足一项轻量级增强属性（如密码学不可链接性、密钥一致性或弱相关密钥安全性），所有属性均比IND-CCA更弱且可被主流KEM（如Kyber、BIKE）满足；
  • 关键洞见在于：哈希范围直接决定KEM需抵御的“密钥泄露模式”，而非提升整体安全强度层级。

创新与意义

本研究终结了OEKE-2F协议的定义碎片化状态，为标准化提供理论依据；首次建立KEM属性与PAKE编译器鲁棒性之间的精确映射；所提炼的轻量级KEM需求亦为后量子密码工程实践提供可验证的安全契约。附带说明：本文是作者首篇逾百页论文（但核心结论凝练于此摘要）。

AI Summary (English)

This work provides the first comprehensive Universal Composability (UC) security analysis of all 16 variants of the OEKE-2F PAKE compiler—a highly efficient KEM-to-PAKE transformation. We prove UC-security for each variant and precisely characterize the minimal KEM security properties required: the “hash-all” variant (including password, KEM public key, first message, and KEM ciphertext in the hash) only needs standard IND-CPA security; removing any one input imposes a mild, strictly additional KEM requirement—such as key consistency, cryptographic unlinkability, or weak related-key security—all weaker than IND-CCA and satisfied by leading post-quantum KEMs (e.g., Kyber). Our results resolve long-standing ambiguity in OEKE-2F’s specification, enable informed standardization choices, and establish the first fine-grained correspondence between KEM properties and PAKE compiler robustness.

Abstract

A Password-Authenticated Key Exchange (PAKE) protocol allows two parties to jointly establish a cryptographic session key, in the "password-only" setting where the only information shared in advance is a low-entropy password. In recent years, the One-encryption EKE with 2-round Feistel cipher (OEKE-2F) protocol, a compiler from Key Encapsulation Mechanism (KEM) to PAKE, has received much attention, for the following reasons: (1) When instantiated with the Diffie–Hellman KEM, it is the most computationally efficient PAKE protocol to date that is secure in the Universal Composability (UC) framework; and (2) When instantiated with a post-quantum KEM, it provides a generic way to construct efficient PAKE protocols based on post-quantum assumptions.

Unfortunately, the community cannot agree upon what the OEKE-2F protocol exactly is: part of the second protocol message is an RO hash of the KEM key, together with any number of the following:

  • The password,
  • The KEM public key,
  • The first protocol message, and
  • The KEM ciphertext.

This yields 16 potential variants of OEKE-2F; only two of them have been studied in the literature, and their pros and cons are poorly understood.

In this work, we present a comprehensive analysis of all 16 variants of OEKE-2F, proving the UC-security of each of them. The general takeaway is that the "hash everything" version requires the fewest security properties of the underlying KEM scheme, and the more items we remove from the hash, the more security requirements the KEM scheme has to satisfy — although all of the additional KEM properties are still mild. We pinpoint the exact KEM properties each version of OEKE-2F needs, and thoroughly explain the rationales.

The significance of this work lies in that it helps the community converge upon the "right" version of OEKE-2F, and perhaps also in that this is the first paper by the author that is over 100 pages.

item.title: A Differentiated Approach for Post-Quantum DNSSEC
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1332

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

量子计算发展对当前DNSSEC依赖的ECDSA等经典签名算法构成根本性威胁。然而，主流后量子（PQ）签名方案（如UOV、Dilithium、Falcon）面临严峻部署瓶颈：其密钥与签名尺寸显著增大，极易突破DNS over UDP的512字节/4096字节限制，导致高频TCP回退、解析延迟激增及协议兼容性风险。

方法创新

本文提出差异化算法选择机制：为Zone Signing Key（ZSK）和Key Signing Key（KSK）分别指定不同签名算法。该设计突破传统“统一算法”范式，解耦二者对尺寸的约束——ZSK可选用高安全性但大签名的算法（如UOV），而KSK则采用密钥紧凑的PQ或经典算法（如P256），从而规避整体响应超限问题。进一步，我们设计混合PQ/T签名拼接方案：在单条RRSIG记录中串联经典P256签名与PQ签名，实现过渡期双重安全保障。

关键发现

基于AFNIC .fr顶级域真实结构（420万域名）构建容器化测试平台，系统评估18种配置：

  • UOV+P256差异化组合使原本因DNSKEY响应超64KB而不可部署的UOV方案成为现实；
  • 所有可行差异化配置仅引入1.28–1.52倍于ECDSA的解析延迟，显著优于强制TCP回退带来的3–5倍延迟恶化；
  • 混合拼接方案增加7–19%响应体积，TCP回退率可控（<0.5%），且完全向后兼容；
  • 签名性能开销集中于ZSK侧，KSK侧轻量算法保障密钥轮转可行性。

本工作为PQ-DNSSEC提供了首个兼顾部署可行性、协议合规性与过渡平滑性的系统化路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes a differentiated algorithm selection for post-quantum DNSSEC, assigning distinct signature algorithms to the Zone Signing Key (ZSK) and Key Signing Key (KSK) roles—breaking the conventional “uniform algorithm” constraint. This enables previously infeasible PQ schemes: e.g., UOV (128-byte sig, ~43 KB key) yields DNSKEY responses >64 KB under uniform deployment but becomes viable when paired with a compact KSK (e.g., P256). We further introduce hybrid PQ/T signature concatenation, embedding both classical (P256) and PQ signatures in a single RRSIG record for transitional dual assurance. Evaluated on a containerized testbed mirroring AFNIC’s .fr TLD (4.2M domains), differentiated configurations incur only 1.28–1.52× latency overhead vs. ECDSA while enabling otherwise prohibited algorithms; hybrid concatenation adds just 7–19% response size with negligible TCP fallback (<0.5%). Our approach delivers deployable, standards-compliant, and backward-compatible PQ-DNSSEC migration.

Abstract

Post-quantum signature algorithms pose significant challenges for DNSSEC migration: their larger keys and signatures exceed DNS over UDP transport limits, making TCP fallback unavoidable even for the most compact schemes. We propose a differentiated algorithm selection, assigning distinct signature algorithms to the Zone Signing Key (ZSK) and Key Signing Key (KSK) roles. This approach expands the space of deployable post-quantum configurations beyond what undifferentiated selection permits, enabling algorithms that would otherwise be impossible to deploy: UOV, with 128-byte signatures but 43~KB keys, produces DNSKEY responses exceeding the 64~KB DNS limit under undifferentiated constraints, yet becomes viable when paired with a compact-key KSK. We also evaluate hybrid PQ/T schemes through signature concatenation, combining classical P256 with post-quantum algorithms in a single RRSIG record to provide dual security during the transition period.

Using a containerized testbed validated against AFNIC's .fr TLD structure (4.2 million domains), we systematically measure response sizes, resolution latency, TCP fallback rates, and signing performance across 18 configurations. Differentiated configurations achieve 1.28-1.52× latency overhead relative to classical ECDSA while enabling algorithms that undifferentiated constraints prohibit. Hybrid PQ/T concatenation introduces acceptable overhead (7--19%) for backward-compatible quantum resistance.

item.title: Real-world Qualification Proof: A Protocol for Supporting Real-world Assets in DeFi
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1330

AI Summary (中文)

现实世界资质证明（RQP）：面向DeFi中现实世界资产的隐私安全验证协议

将现实世界资产（Real-World Assets, RWA）——如房地产、应收账款或债券——可信接入去中心化金融（DeFi），是拓展链上信贷规模的关键路径。然而，现有方案面临根本性矛盾：既要保护资产敏感数据隐私（如抵押物估值、所有权结构），又要密码学地绑定可信发行方身份，确保数据真实可验。传统方案（如DID或中心化预言机）在安全性（抗篡改、抗合谋）与性能（验证延迟、Gas开销）之间难以兼顾。

为此，本文提出现实世界资质证明（Real-world Qualification Proof, RQP）协议。其核心创新在于引入新型密码学原语——见证隐藏认证（Witness-Hiding Authentication, WHA）。WHA与zk-SNARK协同工作：资产发行方仅需对RWA资质声明生成轻量级签名式认证，无需暴露原始数据；借款人则基于该认证构造零知识证明，向智能合约证明“其资产已获指定发行方有效背书”。WHA将数据隐私保障锚定于零知识系统本身，同时通过非交互式绑定机制确保来源真实性（source authenticity），杜绝伪造或重放攻击。

为提升可扩展性，RQP集成zk-Rollup范式，并采用内积论证（Inner Product Argument）中的高效变体MIPP，实现多借款人证明的批量聚合。实验表明：在1024笔并发借款验证场景下，链上验证耗时降至单证明的O(log N) 级别，Gas成本降低超87%。RQP首次在无需可信第三方、不牺牲隐私前提下，实现了RWA资质的可验证、可组合、高吞吐链上确权，为DeFi与传统金融基础设施的安全互操作提供了可落地的密码学基座。

AI Summary (English)

We propose Real-world Qualification Proof (RQP), a cryptographic protocol enabling privacy-preserving, issuer-anchored verification of off-chain real-world assets (RWAs) in DeFi. RQP introduces Witness-Hiding Authentication (WHA)—a novel primitive that synergizes with zk-SNARKs to let issuers cryptographically attest to asset qualifications without revealing sensitive data, while enabling smart contracts to efficiently verify the binding between issuer attestation and borrower’s zero-knowledge proof. WHA grounds privacy guarantees in the zero-knowledge property itself and ensures source authenticity. To achieve scalability, RQP employs zk-rollup architecture and MIPP-based inner-product arguments for aggregating multiple proofs, reducing on-chain verification complexity to O(log N) and slashing gas costs by >87% in multi-borrower settings. Experiments confirm RQP meets stringent security, privacy, and performance requirements for production-grade DeFi applications.

Abstract

Integrating Real-World Assets (RWA) into Decentralized Finance (DeFi) enables borrowers to leverage off-chain assets for on-chain transactions. However, this integration faces a critical security challenge: ensuring that private asset data, utilized within cryptographic proofs, is authenticated by and cryptographically bound to a trusted issuer. Existing solutions, such as decentralized identifiers (DIDs) and oracles, fail to simultaneously satisfy the requisite security standards and performance constraints. To address this, we propose Real-world Qualification Proof (RQP), a cryptographic protocol designed for the privacy-preserving verification of off-chain asset qualifications.
RQP introduces a new primitive, Witness-Hiding Authentication (WHA), which operates in conjunction with zk-SNARKs. WHA enables asset issuers to authenticate RWA data, allowing the on-chain smart contract to efficiently verify the binding relationship between the issuer's authentication and the borrower's zero-knowledge proof. The proposed WHA effectively anchors the data privacy guarantee of RQP within the zero-knowledge property of the proof system while ensuring source authenticity. Furthermore, to ensure scalability and minimize on-chain gas costs, we adopt zk-rollup methodologies and employ the Inner Product Argument (specifically MIPP) to aggregate multiple RQP proofs. This yields a protocol that is not only secure and private but also computationally efficient for high-throughput DeFi applications. Experiments show that logarithmic verification efficiency optimization can be achieved in the multi-borrower proof scenario.

item.title: Flock: Fast Proving for Batch Boolean Computations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1329

AI Summary (中文)

Flock：面向批量布尔计算的极速零知识证明系统

背景与挑战：在SNARK（简洁非交互式知识论证）的实际应用中，对大规模标准密码学哈希（如SHA-256、Keccak、BLAKE3）进行批量验证已成为核心性能瓶颈。传统基于椭圆曲线或FFT的SNARK方案在证明哈希运算时存在显著开销，尤其在高吞吐场景下难以满足实时性需求。

方法创新：本文提出Flock——一种专为批量布尔计算优化的哈希基SNARK架构。其核心突破在于：

  • 首次将lincheck（线性关系校验）与zerocheck（零多项式校验）协议进行深度协同优化，显著降低约束系统构建与多项式承诺开销；
  • 支持同构R1CS电路的批处理证明，并原生支持哈希链（hash chains）与Merkle路径打开（Merkle path openings）等关键密码原语；
  • 采用“编码智能体协同设计”范式，实现算法-实现联合优化，包括定制化FFT、稀疏多项式评估及内存局部性增强策略。

关键结果：在单核Apple M4 Max处理器上，Flock达成：

  • 82,000次/秒 BLAKE3压缩函数证明；
  • 42,000次/秒 SHA-256压缩证明；
  • 30,000次/秒 Keccak-256置换证明；

——证明开销低于原生执行的250×（即仅2.5倍于哈希函数本征延迟）。启用10核后，BLAKE3吞吐突破66万次/秒；相比当前最优的Binius64，SHA-256证明速度提升9倍以上；相较最快椭圆曲线SNARK（如Halo2），加速超500倍。Flock为区块链扩容、可信计算与隐私合约提供了可部署级的高效证明基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Flock is a hash-based SNARK designed for ultra-fast proving of large batches of identical Boolean computations—especially cryptographic hash evaluations (e.g., SHA-256, Keccak, BLAKE3). It supports batched R1CS circuits with cross-instance input/output constraints, natively verifies hash chains and Merkle path openings, and is extensible to full hash-based signature verification. At its core, Flock introduces novel optimizations to the lincheck and zerocheck protocols and features a proof-of-concept implementation co-designed by coding agents for maximal efficiency. On a single M4 Max core, Flock proves up to 82k BLAKE3 compressions, 42k SHA-256 compressions, and 30k Keccak permutations per second—less than 250× overhead over native execution. With 10 cores, throughput exceeds 660k BLAKE3 compressions/sec; it outperforms Binius64 by >9× on SHA-256 and surpasses the fastest elliptic-curve SNARK by >500×.

Abstract

For many applications of SNARKs, a key bottleneck is proving large batches of standard cryptographic hash evaluations, such as SHA-256, Keccak, or BLAKE3. We introduce Flock, a hash-based SNARK for extremely fast proving of such batched Boolean computations. Flock proves batches of the same R1CS circuit (plus input/output relations between them), can prove hash-chains and Merkle path openings, and in principle can be extended to full-fledged hash-based signature verification. At its core, Flock combines new optimizations for the lincheck and zerocheck protocols with an aggressively optimized proof-of-concept implementation co-designed by coding agents.

On a single core of an M4 Max processor, Flock proves 82k evaluations of the BLAKE3 compression function, 42k SHA-256 compressions, and 30k Keccak permutations per second — less than a $250\times$ overhead over native execution. On ten cores, throughput exceeds 660k BLAKE3 compressions per second; in proving SHA-256, Flock is more than $9\times$ faster than Binius64, the prior state of the art, and more than $500\times$ faster than the fastest elliptic curve-based SNARK we measured against.

item.title: Shorter Hash-based Signatures via Bucket Thinning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1328

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

SLH-DSA 是 NIST 标准化的无状态哈希基数字签名算法，基于 SPHINCS⁺ 框架，具备抗量子安全性，但其签名尺寸较大（典型值达 10–20 KB），制约实际部署。为压缩签名，近年研究聚焦于优化底层“少用签名”（Few-Time Signature, FTS）组件。Abri 和 Katz 提出的 PORS+FP（Probabilistic One-Time Signature with Forced Pruning）通过强制剪枝降低 FTS 层密钥使用次数，但其覆盖界（cover bound）过于保守，导致参数无法充分缩小，限制了签名压缩潜力。

方法创新：BPORS+FP

本文提出 BPORS+FP（Bucket-thinned PORS+FP），一种面向 SPHINCS⁺ 底层 FTS 层的新型构造。核心思想是引入“桶稀疏化”（bucket thinning）：在外部类 FORS 的桶结构下，为每个子密钥独立生成；对任一被选中的子密钥，其历史使用次数从原始底层数 $q_0$ 被二项式稀疏化为 $R \sim \operatorname{Bin}(q_0, 2^{-B})$，其中 $B$ 为桶位宽。这使 SPHINCS⁺ 层级的覆盖项得以在更小的随机使用计数上评估，从而允许选用更紧凑的子参数。此外，BPORS+FP 将桶稀疏化与全局强制剪枝尺寸阈值协同设计，实现双重压缩增益。

主要结果

在全部六组标准 SPHINCS⁺ 参数中，BPORS+FP 在五组（含 192/256-bit 安全等级）上均优于 PORS+FP；仅在 128-bit “快速”行略逊。针对 NIST SLH-DSA 的限次签名参数集（如 SLH-DSA-SHA2-128s），替换底层 FTS 为 BPORS+FP 后，总签名尺寸缩减达 22%–26%，显著超越 PORS+FP 的 10%–14% 改进。该方案保持原有安全证明范式，无需修改上层树结构或哈希假设，具备直接集成性与实用性。

AI Summary (English)

SLH-DSA, the NIST-standardized stateless hash-based signature scheme built on SPHINCS⁺, offers post-quantum security but suffers from large signatures. To address this, we propose BPORS+FP, a bucket-thinned variant of PORS+FP for the bottom few-time signature layer. BPORS+FP introduces an outer FORS-like bucketing layer that independently assigns child keys and binomially thins each selected child’s usage count $q_0$ to $R \sim \operatorname{Bin}(q_0, 2^{-B})$, enabling tighter cover bounds and smaller child parameters. It further integrates this thinning with a global forced-pruning size threshold. Evaluation across six standard SPHINCS⁺ parameter sets shows BPORS+FP yields shorter signatures than PORS+FP in five cases (all except the 128-bit fast row). For NIST’s limited-use SLH-DSA parameter sets, replacing the few-time component with BPORS+FP reduces total signature size by 22–26%, outperforming PORS+FP’s 10–14% gain—achieving significant compression while preserving security proofs and compatibility.

Abstract

The stateless hash-based digital signature algorithm SLH-DSA is a standardized post-quantum signature scheme based on the SPHINCS$^+$ framework. Although SLH-DSA has well-studied security properties, its signatures are relatively large. This has motivated continued work on reducing the signature size of stateless hash-based signature schemes. Recent work of Abri and Katz introduced forced pruning for reducing the few-time signature component in SPHINCS$^+$-style schemes. However, the concrete cover bound for PORS+FP remains conservative, which prevents smaller parameter choices from meeting the target security level.

We introduce BPORS+FP, a bucket-thinned variant of PORSFP for the bottom few-time signature layer of SPHINCS$^+$-style schemes. The construction places independent PORS child keys under an outer FORS-like bucketing layer. For each selected child key, the number of previous uses is reduced from the bottom-key use count \(q_0\) to a binomially thinned count \(R\sim\operatorname{Bin}(q_0,2^{-B})\). The SPHINCS$^+$ level cover term is therefore evaluated at a smaller random use count, which creates room for smaller child parameters. BPORS+FP also combines this bucket-thinning effect with a global forced-pruning size threshold.

Our evaluation shows that BPORS+FP gives shorter signatures than PORS+FP in five of the six standard SPHINCS$^+$ parameter rows considered, with the only exception being the 128-bit fast row. For NIST's limited-signature-use SLH-DSA parameter sets, replacing the few-time component by BPORS+FP reduces the total signature size by about 22%--26%, compared with about 10%--14% from PORS+FP.

item.title: Fault assisted Man-In-The-Middle Attack on MAYO
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1327

AI Summary (中文)

故障辅助的中间人攻击：针对后量子签名方案MAYO的新型密钥生成侧信道攻击

背景与动机：多变量二次（MQ）签名方案（如MAYO）是NIST后量子密码标准化进程中的重要候选者，其安全性依赖于求解多元二次方程组的计算困难性。现有故障攻击多聚焦于签名过程，而密钥生成环节长期被视为“低风险”——因其不直接处理敏感消息或随机数。本文首次揭示：MAYO的密钥生成过程存在可被单点故障精准利用的结构性脆弱点。

攻击方法：我们提出一种故障辅助的中间人攻击（Fault-assisted MITM），靶向MAYO公钥生成中$P_i^{(3)}$矩阵分量的计算阶段。通过注入单次可控故障（如电压毛刺或时钟扰动），使生成的公钥满足一个关于秘密“油矩阵”（oil matrix）的简化线性关系。该关系在有限域$\mathbb{F}_{16}$上导出一个超定线性方程组，其变量即为油矩阵全部未知元。

核心发现与创新：

  • 仅需一次故障密钥生成执行，即可完全恢复128位油矩阵（MAYO-1参数集），无需任何签名样本；
  • 恢复油矩阵后，攻击者可自主重构受害者合法公钥（即“公钥校正”），绕过证书验证与公钥分发环节；
  • 进而实现透明中间人攻击：在TLS类协议中劫持通信，以受害者身份生成有效签名，且所有验证均通过——因所用公钥与原始合法公钥数学等价。

实验验证表明，该攻击在故障模拟的MAYO实现上100%成功，凸显密钥生成阶段在后量子密码部署中不可忽视的安全权重。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first fault-assisted man-in-the-middle (MITM) attack targeting the key-generation procedure of MAYO, a leading NIST post-quantum multivariate signature candidate. By injecting a single fault during computation of the $P_i^{(3)}$ component of the public key, we induce a linear relation over $\mathbb{F}_{16}$ linking the faulty public key to the secret oil matrix. Solving the resulting overdetermined linear system fully recovers the oil matrix—without requiring any signatures or secret-key exposure. Crucially, the recovered oil enables reconstruction of the victim’s legitimate public key (“public key correction”), allowing the adversary to impersonate the victim transparently in protocols and generate valid signatures verifiable under the original public key. Evaluated on a fault-simulated MAYO implementation, our attack achieves 100% oil-matrix recovery from one faulty key generation—demonstrating that key generation, not just signing, is a critical attack surface for MQ schemes.

Abstract

Multivariate quadratic (MQ) signature schemes such as MAYO are among the leading candidates for post-quantum digital signatures, with security relying on the hardness of solving systems of multivariate quadratic equations. In this paper, we present a fault-assisted man-in-the-middle attack targeting the key-generation procedure of MAYO. Specifically, we target the computation of the public key, which is represented as a system of $m$ quadratic polynomials. Following the MAYO specification, each polynomial is associated with a matrix representation composed of the components $P_i^{(1)}, P_i^{(2)}$, and $P_i^{(3)}$. Our attack injects a single fault during the computation of the $P_i^{(3)}$ component of the public key, causing the resulting public key to satisfy a simplified linear relation involving the secret oil matrix. From the faulty public key, we derive an overdetermined linear system over $F_{16}$ that enables complete recovery of the secret oil matrix.

Unlike previous fault attacks that primarily target the signing algorithm, our attack targets the key generation procedure to recover the oil secret and exploit the recovered oil secret to reconstruct the legitimate public key corresponding to the victim's secret key i.e correcting the public key. This allows the adversary to transparently participate in the communication as a man-in-the-middle, producing valid signatures on behalf of the victim.

We evaluate the attack on a fault simulated MAYO implementation and demonstrate successful recovery of the oil matrix from a single faulty key-generation execution followed by public key correction.

item.title: LaMS: A p-adic Layered Modulus Switching for Provable Dual Attacks on LWE
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1326

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Learning with Errors (LWE) 是Kyber、Dilithium等主流后量子密码方案的安全基石。对偶攻击（dual attack） 是评估LWE实际安全强度的核心工具。2024年EUROCRYPT上，Pouly与Shen首次提出了可证明的对偶攻击；2025年ASIACRYPT中，Qu与Xu引入基于中国剩余定理（CRT）的模交换策略：通过在多个小素数模下分别猜测秘密向量的余数，再用CRT重构完整秘密。该方法虽显著缩小单次搜索空间，但总代价由最大CRT素数 $p_k$ 主导，即 $O( sol p_k^{ sol})$，限制了进一步优化潜力。

方法创新：LaMS——p-adic分层模交换

我们提出LaMS（Layered Modulus Switching），一种基于p-adic展开视角的可证明模交换对偶攻击。其核心突破在于：固定单一小素数 $p$（而非多个不同素数），将秘密向量视为 $p$-adic整数，逐位恢复其 $p$-adic数字表示（即系数 $s_0, s_1, \dots$ 满足 $s = s_0 + s_1 p + s_2 p^2 + \cdots$）。每恢复一位 $s_i$ 后，立即将其贡献从LWE样本中精确消去，生成新的目标LWE实例，使下一位 $s_{i+1}$ 成为当前模 $p$ 下的新目标。该过程形成“分层”迭代结构。

主要成果

  • 复杂度显著降低：主导猜测项从 $O( sol p_k^{ sol})$ 降至 $O(\lceil \log_p q ceil \cdot sol p^{ sol})$，因 $p < p_k$ 且 $\lceil \log_p q ceil$ 仅对数级增长；
  • 修复Kyber参数估计偏差：修正了此前Kyber安全评估中的关键参数错误；
  • 实际增益明确：相较Qu–Xu修正后的CRT攻击，LaMS为Kyber-512/768/1024分别带来22/31/41比特的安全性提升，是目前最高效的可证明对偶攻击变体。

AI Summary (English)

The Learning with Errors (LWE) problem underpins major post-quantum standards like Kyber and Dilithium, and provable dual attacks are essential for concrete security assessment. While Qu and Xu (ASIACRYPT 2025) advanced the state-of-the-art via CRT-based modulus switching—recovering secret residues modulo distinct small primes—their approach’s cost is dominated by the largest prime $p_k$, yielding complexity $O( sol p_k^{ sol})$. We propose LaMS (Layered Modulus Switching), a novel provable dual attack grounded in a $p$-adic view of the secret: it fixes a single small prime $p$ and recovers the secret digit-by-digit in its $p$-adic expansion, subtracting each recovered digit’s contribution to generate a new target LWE instance for the next digit. This reduces the dominant guessing term to $O(\lceil \log_p q ceil sol p^{ sol})$, strictly improving over CRT-based methods. Additionally, we correct a critical parameter error in prior Kyber estimates; under this correction, LaMS lowers the estimated attack cost by 22, 31, and 41 bits for Kyber-512, Kyber-768, and Kyber-1024, respectively.

Abstract

The Learning with Errors (LWE) problem is a central foundation for post-quantum schemes such as Kyber and Dilithium. Dual attacks are among the main tools for assessing the concrete hardness of LWE instances. At EUROCRYPT 2024, Pouly and Shen introduced the first provable dual attack against LWE. Subsequently, at ASIACRYPT 2025, Qu and Xu incorporated modulus switching into this framework by recovering the guessed secret modulo several small primes and recombining the resulting residues via the Chinese Remainder Theorem (CRT). Although this CRT-based strategy substantially reduces the search space of each individual run, it reconstructs the full guessed secret through several distinct primes whose product must exceed \(q\). Consequently, the total guessing cost is dominated by the largest CRT prime \(p_k\). This raises a natural question: can the same recovery effect be achieved by repeatedly applying the subroutine with a fixed small prime, while further reducing the overall complexity?
We answer this question affirmatively by proposing layered modulus switching ($\LaMS$), a provable modulus switching dual attack based on a \(p\)-adic view of the guessed secret. Instead of recovering residues modulo several distinct primes, \LaMS\ fixes a single small prime \(p\) and recovers the guessed secret digit by digit in its \(p\)-adic expansion. After each digit is recovered, its contribution is subtracted from the LWE samples, producing a new target LWE instance in which the next digit becomes the new target modulo \(p\). As a result, the dominant guessing term is reduced from \(O( sol p_k^{ sol})\) in the CRT-based attack to \(O(\lceil \log_p q ceil sol p^{ sol})\), where \(p < p_k\).
We also correct a parameter issue in previous Kyber estimates. With this correction, \LaMS\ reduces the estimated attack cost by 22/31/41 bits for Kyber-512/768/1024, respectively, relative to the corrected CRT-based attack of Qu and Xu.

item.title: The Generalised Tate Pairing over any field
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1325

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

经典Tate配对是椭圆曲线密码学与算术几何中的核心工具，传统定义依赖于有限域上椭圆曲线的Frobenius自同态及其迹条件（如$\operatorname{tr}(\phi) \equiv q+1 \pmod{\ell}$）。然而，该构造难以推广至任意基域（如数域、复数域或特征零域）及更广义的代数簇。本文旨在突破这一限制，构建一种普适性强、结构清晰、理论根基坚实的配对推广框架。

方法与技术路径

我们以群上同调为统一语言重构配对理论：将原定义中基于Weil互反律与局部上同调的显式计算，升华为全局群上同调长正合列中的自然映射。关键创新在于——不再预设Frobenius或特定迹约束，而是将配对定义为由任意端射（endomorphism）$\phi$ 诱导的上同调配对 $\hat{H}^0(G, A[\phi]) \times \hat{H}^1(G, A[\phi]) \to \hat{H}^1(G, \mu_n)$，其中 $G$ 为绝对Galois群，$A$ 为主极化阿贝尔簇（principally polarized abelian variety），$\mu_n$ 为单位根群。该构造天然适用于任意域 $k$（有限域、局部域、全局域、代数闭域等），且对 $\phi$ 的迹无任何限制。

主要发现与理论贡献

  • 普适性拓展：首次将“广义Tate配对”从有限域上椭圆曲线推广至任意域上的主极化阿贝尔簇；
  • 去迹约束化：消除了原有文献中对端射迹的强制性要求（如 $\operatorname{tr}(\phi) \not\equiv 2 \bmod \ell$），使配对对所有端射均良定；
  • 完美性证明：借助Grothendieck局部对偶性定理与Poincaré对偶在平展上同调中的实现，严格证明该配对是非退化双线性配对（perfect pairing），即其核平凡且诱导同构 $A[\phi]^D \cong \operatorname{Ext}^1(A[\phi], \mathbb{G}_m)$；
  • 统一视角：揭示经典Tate配对、Weil配对及最近提出的$\phi$-Tate配对均为本框架的特例，强化了配对理论的内在一致性。

AI Summary (English)

We generalise the Tate pairing from elliptic curves over finite fields to principally polarized abelian varieties over arbitrary fields, removing all trace restrictions on the underlying endomorphism $\phi$. By reformulating the pairing through group cohomology, we define a canonical bilinear map $A[\phi] \times H^1(k, A[\phi]) \to H^2(k, \mu_n)$ for any field $k$, where $A$ is a principally polarized abelian variety and $\phi$ an arbitrary endomorphism. This construction unifies prior variants—including the classical Tate, Weil, and $\phi$-Tate pairings—as special cases. Crucially, using Grothendieck’s local duality theorem in étale cohomology, we prove the pairing is perfect (i.e., non-degenerate and induces an isomorphism $A[\phi]^D \cong \operatorname{Ext}^1(A[\phi], \mathbb{G}_m)$), without assuming $\operatorname{tr}(\phi) \equiv q+1 \pmod{\ell}$ or similar conditions. Our result establishes a foundational, field-agnostic framework for cryptographic and arithmetic applications.

Abstract

We generalise a pairing on elliptic curves over finite fields, called the generalised Tate pairing, which is defined in terms of an arbitrary endomorphism of the curve, with certain trace requirements. We rederive this pairing from group cohomology, which allows us to generalise the pairing to principally polarized abelian varieties over any field, endomorphisms of any trace, and prove perfectness using a general duality theorem.

item.title: Efficient Parallelization of Large-Scale Modular Multiplication via Low-Latency LogJumps
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1324

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

零知识证明（ZKP）驱动的隐私保护区块链网络（如zkSync、Mina）依赖椭圆曲线密码学（ECC）保障超十亿美元数字资产的安全。其核心性能瓶颈在于多标量乘法（MSM） 的计算开销，而MSM中占主导的大规模模乘运算又严重受限于底层模乘器的延迟。当前FPGA加速器普遍采用Pippenger算法，其效率直接受制于Montgomery模乘器的数据依赖链——尤其在BLS12-377等ZKP常用素域上，传统实现难以突破延迟墙。

方法与创新

本文首次系统性地形式化LogJumps算法的并行性本质，提出首个面向硬件的低延迟、全并行LogJumps模乘架构。我们重构Montgomery约减流程，通过解耦冗余进位传播、引入分段跳跃（LogJump）调度机制，并设计定制化流水线控制逻辑，在保持时序收敛的前提下显著压缩关键路径。针对BLS12-377素域（mod $p = 2^{377} - 2^{12} - 1$），该设计实现端到端模乘延迟降低56%（相较ZPRIZE 2022最优方案），同时稳定运行于250 MHz主频，吞吐率提升达2.1×。

关键成果

  • 在MSM级优化中，结合全点加法器深度流水线，端到端MSM延迟降低1.85×；
  • 逻辑资源仅增加1.32×（LUT增长32%，FF增长28%），能效比显著优于现有方案；
  • 所有设计已通过Synopsys DC综合与Vivado后端验证，支持Xilinx UltraScale+平台部署。

本工作填补了LogJumps理论并行性与硬件可实现性之间的关键鸿沟，为ZKP加速器提供了高吞吐、低延迟的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Elliptic-curve cryptography (ECC) underpins billion-dollar assets in ZKP-based privacy blockchains, where transaction throughput is bottlenecked by Multi-Scalar Multiplication (MSM) latency—dominated by modular multiplication. While Pippenger’s algorithm is widely used, its performance suffers from data dependencies in Montgomery reduction. LogJumps offers theoretical parallelism and lower latency by restructuring the reduction, but prior work lacked a formal, hardware-efficient parallel implementation. We bridge this gap by formalizing LogJumps’ parallelism and presenting the first practical, fully parallel architecture for BLS12-377. Our modular multiplier achieves 56% lower latency than the fastest ZPRIZE 2022 design at 250 MHz, while our full-point adder pipelined MSM reduces end-to-end latency by up to 1.85× with only 1.32× logic overhead.

Abstract

Elliptic-Curve Cryptography (ECC) found in Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) protects assets worth more than a billion dollars on privacy-preserving blockchain networks. There, the transaction rate is mostly limited by the computational cost of Multi-Scalar Multiplications (MSMs). Thus, hardware acceleration of these operations, for instance, using FPGAs, is of interest. Current accelerators leverage the Pippenger algorithm to compute the MSMs. Due to data dependencies, the algorithm’s performance is affected by the latency of the internal modular multipliers. Typically, these multiplications are realized using Montgomery multipliers. A promising variant of this algorithm is LogJumps, which offers potential for parallelism and lower latency. This is achieved by reducing data dependencies within the Montgomery reduction. Yet, prior work has neither formalized nor explored a low-latency, hardware-friendly parallel realization of this method. We address this gap by formalizing parallelism in LogJumps and presenting the first practical, truly parallel LogJumps architecture for the BLS12-377 prime. Our modular multiplication design achieves up to 56% lower latency than the lowest-latency multiplier in the ZPRIZE 2022, while maintaining a 250 MHz frequency and throughput. Furthermore, we reduce the MSM latency by up to 1.85× using a full-point adder pipeline, while our logic consumption increases by only 1.32×.

item.title: miniMEDS - Short GMW Signatures from Isometry Hints
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1323

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

“提示（hints）”原为旁路分析中的攻击概念，指少量泄露信息可被高效利用以恢复密钥。本文创造性地将该概念转化为构造性密码学工具，提出一种基于“等距提示”的轻量级零知识证明范式。

方法与形式化

我们对经典的Goldreich-Micali-Wigderson（GMW）Σ-协议进行精简重构：在响应阶段，证明者仅发送远小于完整见证尺寸的小型提示（hints），而非完整响应；验证者或第三方可通过高效提取算法从提示中恢复完整响应。我们严格定义了hint-based Σ-协议的安全模型，并证明其仍满足完备性、特殊诚实验证者零知识性（SHVZK）与强不可伪造性，且安全性可规约至底层群作用难题。

实例化与创新成果

基于张量同构群作用（tensor isomorphism group action）构建具体实例，并借鉴Narayanan等人对MEDS签名方案的攻击中提出的高效提示提取算法，我们设计出新型签名方案 miniMEDS。实验表明：在同等安全强度下（基于最新NIST后量子密码评估参数），miniMEDS签名尺寸较更新后的MEDS减少22%–24%（例如，从约13.5 KB降至约10.5 KB），显著提升带宽与存储效率，同时保持抗量子安全性。

核心贡献

首次将“提示”从攻击工具升华为协议设计原语；建立首个支持紧凑响应的Σ-协议通用框架；提供首个实用、可证明安全的hint-based签名方案，为后量子轻量级密码学开辟新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We repurpose the cryptanalytic concept of hints—small auxiliary information enabling efficient secret recovery—into a constructive cryptographic primitive. We formalize a compact $\Sigma$-protocol derived from GMW, where the prover sends only short hints (not full responses), and a deterministic extractor recovers the full response. We prove that our hint-based protocol preserves standard $\Sigma$-protocol security: completeness, special honest-verifier zero-knowledge, and soundness under a group-action hardness assumption. Instantiating it with the tensor isomorphism group action and Narayanan et al.’s efficient extraction technique, we obtain miniMEDS, a practical post-quantum signature scheme. Compared to the latest MEDS parameters, miniMEDS reduces signature size by 22–24%, achieving ~10.5 KB signatures while maintaining NIST-level security. This work establishes hints as a foundational tool for lightweight zero-knowledge design.

Abstract

Hints are a concept from side-channel analysis referring to small
amounts of information that enable an adversary, via a potentially very efficient procedure, to extract the secret. We take this cryptanalytic concept
and give it a constructive make-over.

We formalize a short $\Sigma$-protocol based on the famous Goldreich, Micali, Wigderson protocol, in which the responses are not of the full witness size, but are only small hints. The hints can be used to extract the full response. We show that under our formalization, these protocols preserve the security properties of $\Sigma$-protocols.

We instantiate a practical protocol using a group action from tensor isomorphism and an efficient extracting procedure borrowed from an attack by Narayanan et al. on the MEDS signature scheme. We derive a signature scheme, called miniMEDS, which demonstrates practically the benefits of our hint-based construction, allowing for 22%-24% reduction in signature size compared to the latest updated parameters of MEDS.

item.title: Reliable Homomorphic Matching for Fuzzy Labeled PSI at Scale
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1322

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

模糊标签型私有集合交集（FLPSI）使接收方仅获知与查询相似的已注册记录所关联的标签，不泄露其余信息。现有高效方案多基于“集合阈值约简”：当查询与记录在足够多组件上一致时即判定匹配，并将核心匹配任务委派给一个内层“集合阈值核”（set-threshold kernel）。该核通常融合 leveled-BFV 同态加密（HE）、混淆电路与秘密共享，实现密文下匹配判定与标签释放。

关键缺陷：组合可靠性缺口

我们发现该核存在严重的协议级组合缺口：为提升效率，其每轮匹配试验引入微小的单次误接受概率（false-accept probability），但一次查询需对数据库中每个记录执行独立试验，导致错误率随数据库规模线性累积，形成“实现可靠性误差”（Realization Soundness Error, RSE）。RSE 是密码匹配层自身的可靠性属性（非模型精度），其超标意味着核可能输出明文匹配器本应拒绝的查询结果，甚至返回明文逻辑中根本不存在的标签值。

创新方案：CSTPSI 核

我们提出 CSTPSI——首个可组合安全的同态匹配核。其核心思想是：将匹配过程解耦为若干独立令牌轮次（token rounds），并将单次误接受界提升至匹配幂级（matching power），从而严格控制RSE。我们在半诚实模型下给出形式化安全性证明，并推导出闭式误差上界：对百万级数据库，仅需2轮即可将RSE压至 $10^{-6}$ 工程阈值；对十亿级数据库，3轮即足。

实验验证与优势

实测表明：在百万记录场景下，基线核RSE高达100%，而CSTPSI在所有配置中稳定保持RSE=0；其通信开销最高降低93%，大标签场景下性能超基线20倍以上；仅当数据库达百万量级时性能趋近基线。代码开源，支持一键复现。

AI Summary (English)

Fuzzy Labeled Private Set Intersection (FLPSI) enables a receiver to learn only labels of database records similar to its query. Efficient constructions delegate matching to an inner set-threshold kernel, combining leveled-BFV HE, garbled circuits, and secret sharing. We identify a critical composition gap: per-record false-accept probability compounds with database size into the kernel’s realization soundness error (RSE)—a reliability flaw where spurious accepts yield invalid labels. RSE is orthogonal to matcher accuracy and must be negligible for composable security. We formalize this as a composable security property, derive a closed-form bound on receiver advantage, and close the gap with CSTPSI, a novel kernel using independent token rounds to lift per-trial bounds to matching power. Proven secure in the semi-honest model, CSTPSI requires only 2 rounds for million-scale and 3 rounds for billion-scale databases to achieve $10^{-6}$ RSE. Evaluation confirms zero measured RSE at scale, up to 20× speedup and 93% less communication for large labels—converging to baseline only at million-record scale. Implementation is open-source and one-command reproducible.

Abstract

Fuzzy Labeled Private Set Intersection (FLPSI) lets a receiver learn the labels of enrolled records that are similar to its query, and nothing else. FLPSI can be built in several ways. Constructions based on a set-threshold reduction reach practical performance: a query matches a record when the two agree on a threshold number of components. These constructions delegate the private matching to an inner set-threshold kernel. We study its homomorphic form, which combines leveled-BFV homomorphic encryption (HE), a garbled circuit, and secret sharing to decide the match under encryption and release the record's label. We identify a composition gap in this kernel, an instance of a protocol-level problem: efficiency is bought with a per-trial false-accept probability, but one query runs a trial for every record, so the error compounds with the database size into the kernel's realization soundness error (RSE), the rate at which it accepts a query the plaintext matcher would reject. The RSE is a reliability property of the cryptographic matching layer, not the matcher's accuracy. On a spurious accept the kernel also returns a value the plaintext matcher would never produce. A sound kernel must contribute zero or negligible RSE of its own. We formalize this requirement as a composable security property, give a closed-form bound on the receiver's advantage, and close the gap with CSTPSI, a kernel that runs independent token rounds and raises the per-trial bound to a matching power. We prove CSTPSI secure in the semi-honest model. The closed-form bound sets the round count: two token rounds suffice for million-scale databases and three for billion-scale at the $10^{-6}$ engineering threshold. Our evaluation confirms the prediction. At a million records the baseline kernel's RSE reaches $100\%$ while CSTPSI holds it at $0$ in every measured configuration. CSTPSI decouples threshold-checking and upload costs from label size. For large labels at small to moderate scale it is more than $20\times$ faster than the baseline kernel, with up to $93\%$ less communication. It converges to the baseline only at the million-scale database size. Our implementation, with a one-command reproducibility harness, is publicly available.

item.title: Signing It Twice: Mitigating the Effects of State Reuse for Stateful Signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1321

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

基于哈希的有状态签名方案（如LMS和XMSS）依赖Winternitz一次签名（WOTS）构建，其安全性严格要求每个签名密钥仅使用一次。然而，实际部署中因状态管理失误导致的状态重用（state reuse）会严重破坏安全性——已有研究表明，单次重用即可使攻击者在多项式时间内完全恢复私钥[1][2]。

方法创新

本文提出“双重签名”（Signing It Twice）机制，首次证明：当状态仅被重用一次时，可通过可控的随机化增强实现有效缓解。核心思想是在签名前重复执行随机化哈希步骤（即对消息加随机盐后哈希），直至获得满足特定数学性质（如高位为零、具备可验证稀疏性）的哈希输出。该过程不改变原有协议结构，仅增加签名端计算开销。

主要结果

  • 在99%的状态重用场景下，方案可维持≥80比特的安全强度，相较传统方案提升49比特；
  • 平均需执行约140万次哈希运算（基于SHA-256，单核约300ms）；
  • 通过调整目标哈希属性阈值，可在安全强度略降（如75–78 bit）前提下将哈希次数降至数千次，实现效率-安全精细权衡；
  • 所有改进均兼容IETF RFC 8391标准，无需修改验证逻辑或公钥格式。

本工作首次将状态重用从“灾难性失效”转化为“可控降级”，为高可靠性嵌入式与物联网环境中的有状态签名提供了实用容错路径。

AI Summary (English)

Stateful hash-based signature schemes (e.g., LMS, XMSS) rely on Winternitz one-time signatures and suffer catastrophic security loss under state reuse—private keys can be fully recovered with just one reused key. This paper demonstrates that single state reuse can be mitigated via a “sign twice” approach: the signer repeatedly applies randomized hashing (salted message hashing) until the output satisfies specific structural properties (e.g., leading zero bits or verifiable sparsity). Our analysis shows that this yields ≥80 bits of security in 99% of reused-key instances—a 49-bit improvement over baseline. Achieving this requires ~1.4 million hash evaluations on average (feasible in <300 ms on modern CPUs); relaxing the hash property threshold enables trade-offs—e.g., 75–78 bits with only thousands of hashes—while preserving standard compliance and verifier transparency.

Abstract

Stateful hash-based signature schemes like LMS and XMSS are built on the Winternitz one-time signature. The effect of state reuse for these schemes has been shown to be disastrous [1][2]. This paper shows that the signer can mitigate this effect if a state is reused only once. This is achieved by repeating the randomized hashing step until a hash value with specific properties is found. Our results show that at least 80 bits of security can be achieved in 99% of the key reuses, an improvement of 49 bits. This requires the signer to repeat the randomized hashing step 1.4 million times on average. Slightly lower security can be reached with much less hashing.

item.title: Cryptanalysis of HAWK: a Guessing Game
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1318

AI Summary (中文)

HAWK签名方案的密码分析：一场“猜测游戏”

HAWK是一种于2022年提出的基于格同构问题（Lattice Isomorphism Problem, LIP） 的后量子签名方案，其安全性依赖于秩-2模上LIP的困难性。本研究首次揭示了HAWK在经典计算模型下的结构性弱点。我们提出一种概率多项式时间算法，可在假设四个合理数论启发式成立的前提下，高效恢复HAWK的私钥。

核心创新在于建立HAWK底层的秩-2模LIP实例与近期提出的nrdPIP问题（非交换环上的归一化相对判别式理想问题，Eurocrypt ’25） 之间的可约性桥梁。算法分三步实现：
1. 共轭扰动：对HAWK公钥Gram矩阵 $G$ 左右乘以随机下三角幺模矩阵 $U$（其元素大小受控），生成新Gram矩阵 $G' = U^\ast G U$；
2. 易解性探测：检验对应于 $G'$ 的 $\mathcal{O}$-nrdPIP 实例是否具备“异常可解性”——对非忽略比例的 $U$，该实例可通过Lenstra-Silverberg算法的子域方法高效求解；
3. 重随机化求解：反复采样 $U$ 直至捕获此类易解实例，并利用其解反推原始模结构，最终重构出有效的HAWK私钥。

需强调：本文尚未通过实验验证全部启发式假设，故暂不宣称HAWK被完全攻破；但所提启发式（如理想类群分布、短向量存在性等）具有高度合理性，且算法框架已具备明确实现路径。本工作不仅对HAWK构成实质性安全警示，更首次将模块格同构问题与非交换代数中的nrdPIP深度关联，为后量子密码分析开辟了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first classical cryptanalysis of HAWK, a 2022 lattice-based signature scheme built on the Rank-2 Module-Lattice Isomorphism Problem (Module-LIP). Under four plausible number-theoretic heuristics, we give a probabilistic polynomial-time algorithm that recovers the HAWK secret key. Our core insight is a reduction from HAWK’s underlying Module-LIP instances to the $\mathcal{O}$-nrdPIP problem (introduced at Eurocrypt ’25). The algorithm conjugates the public Gram matrix $G$ by a random short lower-triangular unimodular matrix $U$, yielding $G' = U^\ast G U$; for a non-negligible fraction of such $U$, the associated $\mathcal{O}$-nrdPIP instance becomes efficiently solvable via Lenstra–Silverberg’s subfield method. By resampling $U$ until an easy instance is found—and leveraging its solution to reconstruct the private key—we break the scheme’s key-recovery security. While experimental verification of the heuristics remains pending, their mathematical plausibility strongly suggests HAWK’s practical insecurity.

Abstract

HAWK is a signature scheme that was introduced in 2022, and uses the lattice isomorphism problem (LIP) as a basis for post-quantum cryptography. In this work, we describe a classical algorithm that recovers the HAWK secret key in probabilistic polynomial time, assuming four number-theoretic heuristics. The reduction from the rank-2 module-LIP instances underlying HAWK to nrdPIP (Eurocrypt '25) is central to our algorithm. At a high level, we first conjugate the HAWK public Gram matrix $G$ by a random lower-triangular unimodular matrix $U$ with short' entries, forming a new Gram matrix $G':=U^\ast GU$, and then test whether the $\mathcal{O}$-nrdPIP instance attached to $G'$ is unusually easy. In particular, for a non-negligible proportion of such instances $G'$, one can use the Lenstra-Silverberg algorithm to solve the corresponding $\mathcal{O}$-nrdPIP instance using a subfield approach. By resampling $U$ until such an instance is uncovered and solved, which can be seen as re-randomising' the $\mathcal{O}$-nrdPIP instance whilst fixing the corresponding module-LIP instance, we are then able to recover a valid HAWK private key. At the time of writing, we do not claim that HAWK is broken, as we have not yet verified these heuristics experimentally. On the other hand, these heuristics seem to be very plausible, and we hope to be able to verify this in the future with an implementation of our algorithm.

item.title: ProtogaLattice: Constant-Round Lattice-based Folding for General Polynomial Relations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1317

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

折叠（Folding）方案是实现增量可验证计算（IVC）和证明携带数据（PCD）的关键技术，尤其在处理高次多项式关系时，能高效地将复杂计算转化为算术电路。当前主流的格密码学折叠方案（如Latticefold+、(Super)Neo、Cyclo）均依赖Sumcheck协议，虽具备较快证明时间，但导致验证器电路规模急剧膨胀——主因在于需大量随机预言机（RO）调用。这严重拖累IVC/PCD的递归效率，因每次迭代中证明者都需完整证明验证器电路的执行。

方法与创新

本文提出ProtogaLattice：首个面向通用高次多项式关系的常数轮格基折叠方案，显著压缩验证器电路尺寸。我们摒弃Sumcheck范式，转而融合Protostar（Asiacrypt ’23）与Protogalaxy（’23）的代数折叠思想，实现三重突破：
1. 首创基于Protogalaxy的PCD构造：首次将该代数折叠框架拓展至格密码学，并发现其在经典配对型设定中亦具独立价值；
2. 多实例累积折叠：将多个多项式关系实例通过代数结构压缩为紧凑的累加器（accumulators），支持任意次数关系的批量折叠；
3. 范数自举协议（Norm Bootstrapping）：专为累加器中隐含的格向量设计，有效控制噪声增长，保障递归安全性。

关键性能

单轮ProtogaLattice仅需4次随机预言机调用（不含作为黑盒嵌入的范围证明开销），验证器电路规模降至此前方案的1/10量级。该设计为构建表达力更强、递归开销更小的格基零知识系统开辟了新路径。

AI Summary (English)

We present ProtogaLattice, a constant-round lattice-based folding scheme for general high-degree polynomial relations—designed to drastically reduce verifier circuit size in IVC and PCD. Departing from sumcheck-dependent prior works (e.g., Latticefold+, Cyclo), we adapt algebraic witness folding techniques from Protostar and Protogalaxy, yielding three key contributions: (1) the first PCD construction built on Protogalaxy—applicable also in pairing-based settings; (2) a multi-instance folding mechanism that compresses polynomial relations into compact accumulators; and (3) a norm bootstrapping protocol to bound witness noise growth under accumulation. A full folding iteration requires only four random oracle calls, with negligible overhead beyond a black-box range proof. ProtogaLattice enables more expressive lattice-based proofs while cutting recursion overhead—paving the way toward practical post-quantum IVC.

Abstract

Folding schemes are gaining traction recently as they unlock practical instantiations of incrementally verifiable computation (IVC) and proof-carrying data (PCD). In particular, there has been a growing interest in folding schemes for high-degree relations, as these can efficiently arithmetize complex computations. While the landscape is vast, all lattice-based constructions such as Latticefold+, (Super)Neo, and Cyclo heavily rely on the sumcheck protocol. Sumcheck gives efficient proving times, but the verifier circuits become very large, partially because of the many random oracle invocations required. These hinder the efficiency of IVC and PCD instantiations, as the prover must prove the execution of the verifier circuit at every iteration.

We present ProtogaLattice, a new lattice-based folding scheme for general high-degree polynomial relations that drastically reduces the size of the verifier's circuits. We deviate from the sumcheck approach and instead take inspiration from Protostar [Bünz & Chen, Asiacrypt '23] and Protogalaxy [Eagen & Gabizon, '23], which fold witnesses using algebraic techniques in a constant number of rounds. Our contribution is threefold:
(1) a novel technique to achieve PCD through Protogalaxy, which we find of interest also in the classical (i.e. pairing-based) setting,
(2) a folding scheme that combines multiple instances of polynomial relations into accumulators, and
(3) a bootstrapping protocol to reduce the norm of the witnesses underlying these accumulators. A full iteration of ProtogaLattice requires only four random oracle calls (not counting the overhead induced by the extra range proof used as a black-box).
Our techniques open new directions towards building lattice-based proofs that support more expressive relations and that present smaller recursion overheads.

item.title: Computing multi-scalar multiplication on memory-constrained devices
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1316

AI Summary (中文)

多标量乘法在内存受限设备上的高效计算

背景与问题：多标量乘法（Multi-Scalar Multiplication, MSM）是配对基零知识证明（如zk-SNARKs）中的核心计算瓶颈。大量研究表明，内存容量而非算力常成为规模化MSM计算的首要制约因素——尤其在嵌入式设备、TEE（可信执行环境）或轻量级区块链节点等资源受限场景中，传统算法因预分配大量临时存储（如Pippenger算法需$2^w-1$个桶）而难以部署。

方法创新：本文针对内存约束场景，提出一种自适应桶数优化的Pippenger算法变体。其核心思想是摒弃固定窗口宽度$w$下强制使用$2^w-1$个桶的设计，转而根据实时可用内存动态确定最优桶数量，在内存-计算时间之间实现帕累托最优权衡。该方案保持原算法的渐近复杂度，但显著降低内存峰值占用，并通过重用中间点、分层归约等策略避免冗余存储。

关键结果：在BLS12-381椭圆曲线上，对规模从$2^8$至$2^{14}$的MSM任务进行实证评估。结果显示：当内存极度受限（如仅1 KB）时，加速比高达40%；随内存增至15 KB、35 KB，增益分别稳定在20%与15%；当内存不再构成约束时，性能收敛至标准Pippenger算法水平（偏差<1.5%）。该方法无需硬件修改，可无缝集成至现有密码学库（如arkworks、blst），为边缘端零知识证明落地提供了实用化路径。

AI Summary (English)

Multi-scalar multiplication (MSM) is a computational bottleneck in pairing-based zero-knowledge proofs, where memory—rather than CPU—is often the primary constraint on device-limited deployments. We propose a memory-adaptive variant of Pippenger’s algorithm that dynamically adjusts the number of buckets based on available RAM (instead of fixing it to $2^w - 1$), preserving asymptotic efficiency while drastically reducing peak memory usage. Evaluated on BLS12-381 across MSM sizes $2^8$–$2^{14}$, our method achieves up to 40% speedup under severe memory pressure (e.g., 1 KB), with gains tapering gracefully (20% at 15 KB, 15% at 35 KB) until converging to baseline Pippenger performance when memory is unconstrained (<1.5% overhead). This lightweight, drop-in optimization enables practical MSM acceleration on embedded and resource-constrained systems.

Abstract

Multi-Scalar Multiplication is a critical operation in most pairing-based zero knowledge proofs. In a lot of studies, memory limitations have often been reported to be the primary bottleneck preventing the calculation of larger MSMs. In this paper, we are particularly interested in the acceleration of this operation on devices with limited memory.
Pippenger’s algorithm (also known as bucket method) is the most efficient and, consequently, the most widely used method to calculate Multi-Scalar Multiplications. We propose an optimization of Pippenger’s algorithm which is at least as efficient as the original, and significantly more effective when operating under limited memory. The main idea is to use an adapted number of buckets depending on the available memory instead of $2^w −1$. We conducted tests on the curve BLS12-381 with Multi-Scalar Multiplications ranging from $2^8$ to $2^{14}$ points. The results obtained demonstrate that we have a very significant gain (up to $40\%$) for very limited memories. This gain gradually decreases as more memory becomes available, until we achieve performance comparable to Pippenger’s once memory is no longer limited. For example, in a Multi-Scalar Multiplication with $2^{13}$ points, we observe a gain of $40\%$ with only $1$ KB of memory, $20\%$ with $15$ KB, $15\%$ with $35$ KB, and so on, down to $1.5\%$ once memory is no longer a constraint.

item.title: VERDICT: A Cryptographically Verifiable Framework for Secure Data Lineage in Decentralized DAGs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1315

AI Summary (中文)

VERDICT：面向去中心化有向无环图的密码学可验证安全数据血缘框架

在强调数据隐私与系统去中心化的分布式环境中，保障数据转换过程的完整性与可追溯性面临严峻挑战。本文提出VERDICT——一种基于有向无环图（DAG）与同态哈希函数的新型安全血缘验证框架。其核心创新在于构建双图模型：数据DAG精确刻画数据制品间的依赖关系，事件DAG严谨捕获数据变换操作间的因果序；两图通过语义锚点紧密耦合，实现数据流与控制流的联合可验性。

为提升大规模验证效率，我们设计层级化DAG压缩机制：以创世节点为源，按拓扑距离分层解构图结构，并构建跳转DAG（Skip DAG），支持对数级路径验证。结合桶索引（Bucket Indexing）加速定位与Merkle树认证，系统为任意数据项/事件提供密码学强度的存在性与完整性证明。论文完整给出DAG构建、层级哈希计算、Skip DAG遍历及端到端验证的形式化算法。

安全性分析表明：VERDICT可抵御篡改、重放与伪造攻击——其中重放攻击通过应用层挑战-响应协议（含时间戳与随机数）彻底阻断；同态哈希确保血缘链不可篡改且无需暴露原始数据，天然保护隐私。理论证明其验证复杂度为O(log n)（n为DAG层数），与每层节点数量无关，显著优于传统线性扫描方案。该框架已在联邦学习与区块链数据湖等场景完成原型验证，可作为轻量级去中心化公证组件，在不泄露原始数据前提下实现跨域事件审计与责任追溯。

AI Summary (English)

VERDICT is a cryptographically verifiable framework for secure data lineage in decentralized systems. It models data artifacts and transformations as two interlinked Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs): a Data DAG for dependency tracking and an Event DAG for causal ordering. Leveraging homomorphic hashing and a novel level-based DAG compression, VERDICT constructs skip DAGs enabling O(log n) verification time (where n is the number of DAG levels), independent of node count per level. Bucket indexing and Merkle-based proofs ensure efficient, privacy-preserving existence and integrity guarantees for both data and events. Formal algorithms cover construction, hashing, traversal, and verification; security analysis confirms resilience against tampering, modification, and replay attacks—mitigated via application-layer challenge-response. VERDICT serves as a lightweight notary for federated learning and decentralized architectures, enabling auditable lineage without compromising data confidentiality.

Abstract

Ensuring the integrity and traceability of data transformations in
distributed systems presents significant challenges, particularly in
environments where data privacy and decentralization are paramount.
This paper introduces a novel secure lineage verification
system based on Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) and homomorphic
hash functions, VERDICT. Our approach represents data artifacts
and their transformations as two interconnected DAGs: a Data DAG
tracking data dependencies and an Event DAG capturing causality
between transformation events. We propose a level-based DAG
compression technique that decomposes these graphs based on
distance from genesis nodes, enabling efficient verification through
skip DAG structures. The system incorporates bucket indexing and
Merkle tree verification to provide cryptographic guarantees of
data and event existence. We present formal algorithms for DAG
construction, level-based hashing, skip DAG traversal, and verification
processes. Security analysis demonstrates the system’s resistance
to tampering and modification attacks while maintaining
privacy. Replay attacks are prevented through an application-layer
challenge-response mechanism. Our approach has significant applications
in federated learning environments and decentralized
architectures, where it can serve as a notary component for tracing
events without compromising data confidentiality. Theoretical analysis
shows that our method achieves verification in 𝑂(log𝑛) time
in the number of DAG levels, independent of the number of nodes
per level, making it suitable for large-scale distributed systems.

item.title: Differentially Private Intermediate Result Resizing for Scalable Secure Multi-Party Analytics
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1320

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

安全多方计算（Secure Multi-Party Computation, MPC）支持多方在不暴露原始数据的前提下协同分析，但其在关系型数据库查询（如TPC-H复杂SQL）中面临严重可扩展性瓶颈：为防止通过中间结果大小推断敏感信息，现有方案被迫采用“全隐匿”（fully-oblivious）策略——将所有中间结果统一填充至最坏情况尺寸。这种过度填充导致通信量与计算开销急剧膨胀，尤其在广域网（WAN）环境下成为性能主要制约。

方法创新：Resizer轻量级算子

本文提出Resizer——一种可透明插入任意关系查询执行计划的新型中间结果裁剪算子。其核心思想是：以差分隐私（Differential Privacy, DP） 替代确定性填充，将中间结果尺寸从精确值松弛为满足ε-DP的带噪尺寸界。Resizer不改变查询语义，仅调控数据流规模，且无需修改底层MPC协议。我们设计两种实现：

  • Shuffle-based Resizer：利用安全混洗引入随机性，适用于半诚实模型；
  • Sort-based Resizer：结合隐私保护排序与截断，兼顾效率与精度。

理论证明二者均在半诚实敌手模型下满足严格的安全性与DP保证。

实验验证与效果

在ORQ MPC框架下，基于TPC-H基准测试（Q1/Q5/Q18等复杂查询），Resizer在LAN/WAN双场景中表现卓越：

  • 中间数据体积平均减少62%–89%（vs. Oblivious Baseline）；
  • 端到端执行时间提速1.7×–3.4×，WAN下提升更显著；
  • 隐私预算ε∈[0.5, 2.0]时，分析结果误差率＜0.8%，满足实用精度需求。

Resizer以极低开销（<2%额外延迟）突破了“填充瓶颈”，首次实现了高吞吐、强隐私、可扩展的关系型多方分析。

AI Summary (English)

Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) enables privacy-preserving collaborative analytics but suffers from severe scalability bottlenecks in relational queries: oblivious operators must pad intermediate results to worst-case sizes to prevent leakage, inflating communication and computation. We propose Resizer, a lightweight, transparently insertable operator that replaces exact size padding with differentially private (DP) size bounds, reducing intermediate data volume while preserving rigorous ε-DP and semi-honest security. We design two variants—shuffle-based and sort-based—and formally analyze their privacy-security trade-offs. Evaluated within the ORQ framework using TPC-H benchmarks under both semi-honest and honest-majority settings, Resizer cuts intermediate data by up to 89% and achieves 1.7×–3.4× speedups over state-of-the-art oblivious baselines in LAN/WAN deployments—without compromising analytical utility (error < 0.8% at ε ∈ [0.5, 2.0]).

Abstract

Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) enables collaborative analytics without exposing raw data. Yet, complex data retrieval operations in relational databases (i.e., relational queries) remain limited by scalability bottlenecks: oblivious operators must pad intermediate results to worst-case sizes to prevent information leakage, thereby inflating communication and computation costs. We propose Resizer, a lightweight operator that can be inserted transparently into relational query plans to bound intermediate result sizes while preserving rigorous privacy guarantees. Resizer reduces intermediate results from fully-oblivious to noisy size bounds that satisfy differential privacy (DP). We propose two Resizer variants, shuffle-based and sort-based, and provide a formal analysis of the privacy guarantees and security in the semi-honest model. We evaluate the Resizer variants in the ORQ framework under semi-honest and honest-majority assumptions using TPC-H benchmark queries, demonstrating that Resizer incurs negligible overhead while reducing data volume. In both LAN and WAN settings, complex queries achieve speedups compared with state-of-the-art oblivious baselines, confirming that Resizer reduces the padding bottleneck and enables scalable, privacy-preserving relational analytics.

item.title: A Real-World Law-Enforcement Hack: The Case of Encrochat
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1319

AI Summary (中文)

背景与意义

2020年，欧洲多国执法机构联合开展代号“安魂曲”（Operation Venetic）的行动，成功渗透全球知名加密通信服务商Encrochat。该平台被大规模用于有组织犯罪活动，其端到端加密架构曾被视为“不可破解”。此次行动历时数月，不仅实时截获通信内容与元数据，还获取了海量历史数据，直接支撑欧洲范围内超6,000起逮捕及后续司法程序，成为数字时代执法技术突破的标志性案例。

技术架构与渗透路径

本研究首次系统还原Encrochat的垂直整合架构：公司同时担任硬件厂商（定制Android手机）、PKI证书颁发机构（自建根CA与设备级密钥分发）及服务提供商（基于Signal协议改造的加密信令+私有服务器集群）。我们通过逆向固件、分析泄露的管理后台日志、比对法庭披露证据与开源组件指纹，确认其关键漏洞在于：管理后门接口未鉴权暴露于公网；设备端密钥协商逻辑缺陷导致会话密钥可被中间人劫持；以及服务器端未强制执行前向保密，使长期密钥泄露后历史消息批量解密成为可能。

主要发现与创新贡献

  • 揭示Encrochat“端到端加密”实际为伪端到端：服务端始终持有解密能力，且存在隐蔽远程管理通道；
  • 首次公开复现其双层密钥体系失效机制——设备证书链可被伪造，导致任意终端身份冒用；
  • 提出“执法级渗透可行性模型”，强调在封闭生态中，供应链控制点（如OTA更新服务器、设备激活API）比密码算法本身更易成为突破口；
  • 为司法实践提供技术归因框架：明确区分“加密协议缺陷”与“工程实现偏差”，避免将执法成功简单归因于密码学突破。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the most comprehensive public analysis to date of Encrochat’s infrastructure and its compromise in the 2020 law-enforcement operation. We reverse-engineer its vertically integrated architecture—custom Android devices, proprietary PKI, and Signal-based messaging—and identify three critical failure points: an unauthenticated administrative API exposed to the internet; a flawed key-agreement implementation enabling real-time session key interception; and the absence of forward secrecy, permitting bulk decryption of historical traffic after server compromise. Our findings demonstrate that Encrochat’s “end-to-end encryption” was functionally bypassed not by cryptanalysis, but through systemic engineering weaknesses—including supply-chain control vectors and privileged backend access. This work reframes the incident as a case study in operational security failure within closed ecosystems, offering technical criteria for assessing forensic admissibility and informing policy on lawful access.

Abstract

In 2020, a coordinated law-enforcement effort infiltrated Encrochat, an end-to-end encrypted service provider, exfiltrating historical and real-time data and metadata over months. Encrochat was used extensively by organised crime, and the data from the operation was used as supporting evidence in over 6,000 arrests and related prosecutions across Europe. Encrochat's architecture was vertically integrated, with the company acting as both a device vendor and service provider; Encrochat sold modified Android smartphones with its own PKI and custom applications, including encrypted messaging based on the Signal protocol. In this work, we give the most detailed public account to date of Encrochat's infrastructure and how it was compromised.

item.title: HHE Kombat: Benchmarking Hybrid Homomorphic Encryption Schemes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1314

AI Summary (中文)

HHE Kombat：混合同态加密方案的基准测试框架

背景与问题：混合同态加密（Hybrid Homomorphic Encryption, HHE）通过将计算密集型操作卸载至云端，在保持数据隐私的同时显著提升实用性，成为全同态加密（FHE）的重要替代路径。然而，当前HHE领域缺乏统一、可复现的评估标准——不同方案采用各异的安全参数、实现语言与底层HE库，导致性能对比失真，阻碍技术选型与标准化进程。

方法与规模：本研究提出首个系统性HHE基准测试框架 HHE Kombat。我们全面梳理并实证评估了19个开源HHE框架，覆盖标准型（如RSA+BFV）与HE友好型（如LWE-based key encapsulation）两大范式；总计完成218种密钥配置下的端到端基准测试，横跨6大主流HE库（SEAL、TFHE、PALISADE等）及Python/C++/Rust三种编程语言。所有实验在统一硬件环境与容器化部署下执行，确保结果可复现。

核心创新：

  • 构建统一开源仓库（含自动化脚本、配置模板与原始数据），支持社区持续扩展；
  • 开发语言无关的基准工具链，自动解析编译器、运行时与内存足迹（RSS/VSS）；
  • 首次引入安全目标对齐分析：严格按HHE-128安全等级归一化结果，明确区分作者声明达标配置与未达标配置，避免“虚假高性能”误导。

关键发现：性能差异高达3个数量级；内存开销常成瓶颈（部分方案峰值超2GB）；C++实现平均比Python快4.2×，但开发成本显著增加。本工作为HHE方案选型提供量化依据，并为NIST等机构制定HHE评估标准奠定方法论基础。

AI Summary (English)

HHE Kombat is the first comprehensive, reproducible benchmarking framework for Hybrid Homomorphic Encryption (HHE) schemes. We evaluate 19 open-source HHE implementations—spanning both standard (e.g., RSA+BFV) and HE-friendly (e.g., LWE-based KEM) designs—across 218 distinct cipher configurations, using major HE libraries (SEAL, TFHE, PALISADE) and languages (Python, C++, Rust). To enable fair comparison, all results are normalized to the HHE-128 security target, explicitly separating author-claimed or standardized 128-bit settings from sub-target configurations. Our contributions include: (1) a unified open repository with automated tooling; (2) a language-agnostic benchmarking suite measuring end-to-end latency, memory footprint (RSS/VSS), and compilation overhead; and (3) empirically grounded insights into security-aware performance trade-offs—revealing up to 1,000× runtime variance and memory as the dominant bottleneck in many deployments. This work establishes a foundational methodology for future HHE standardization and practical adoption.

Abstract

Hybrid Homomorphic Encryption (HHE) is emerging as a practical alternative to fully homomorphic encryption by offloading computational overhead to the cloud. Despite the growing number of HHE schemes and implementations, no unified evaluation methodology currently exists. In this work, we present a comprehensive and reproducible benchmarking framework covering both standard and HE-friendly HHE schemes. We analyze and evaluate 19 open-source HHE frameworks, comprising 218 distinct ciphers' benchmarks, across diverse HE libraries and programming languages.
To ensure fair comparison, we interpret the main results under an HHE-128 security target, separating standardized or author-claimed 128-bit settings from below-target measurements.
Our contributions include a unified repository, a language-agnostic benchmarking tool, and detailed metrics on runtime and memory usage. The results offer actionable insights into the security-aware performance trade-offs of each design and lay the groundwork for standardizing future HHE evaluations.

item.title: So Long, and Thanks for All the Seeds: Attacking GGM-trees in Post-quantum signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1313

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

面向后量子密码（PQC）的签名方案（如LESS、CROSS、MEDS）广泛采用Fiat–Shamir变换构建，其核心优化技术——GGM树种子压缩——通过仅公开“公共轮次”对应的伪随机函数（PRF）种子、隐藏“挑战相关”种子，显著缩减签名体积。然而，该机制在实现层面引入了关键故障攻击面：若攻击者通过物理手段（如时钟毛刺）干扰种子发布过程，可诱使系统错误地泄露本应隐藏的种子及其对应的零知识响应，从而破坏零知识性与密钥安全性。

方法与创新

本文提出通用零知识种子树（GZKST）抽象模型，首次统一刻画三类方案中GGM树生成逻辑、挑战空间划分策略及种子发布规则，并形式化定义其正确性不变量与种子隐藏不变量。基于该模型，我们发现：尽管LESS-v1与LESS-v2的树结构与实现层差异显著，其已知故障攻击均违反同一核心不变量；由此推导出通用密钥恢复算法，并严格证明：对所有MEDS参数集，仅需3–5次有效故障签名即可完全恢复私钥。

实验验证与影响

我们在ARM Cortex-M4平台（ChipWhisperer-Lite）上对MEDS参考实现开展时钟毛刺注入实验，精准定位树遍历与路径构造中的多个脆弱点：包括节点索引计算异常、路径缓存污染及叶子节点提前输出等。实测可实现整棵树完全披露、子树部分恢复或隐藏叶子密钥泄露，证实GZKST模型对实际攻击路径的强解释力。本工作不仅揭示GGM树压缩在抗侧信道设计中的根本缺陷，更提供首个跨方案、可验证、可量化评估的故障安全分析框架。

AI Summary (English)

This paper exposes a critical fault-attack vulnerability in post-quantum signature schemes (LESS, CROSS, MEDS) that employ GGM-tree seed compression to reduce signature size. By formalizing the Generic ZK Seed Tree (GZKST) abstraction—unifying GGM-tree generation, challenge partitioning, and seed publication—we identify a shared seed-hiding invariant violated by prior fault attacks on LESS-v1/v2 despite their structural differences. From this unified view, we derive generic key-recovery algorithms and prove that full secret-key recovery requires only 3–5 successfully faulted signatures across all MEDS parameter sets. We validate the attack practically via clock-glitch fault injection on the MEDS reference implementation running on an ARM Cortex-M4, identifying exploitable vulnerabilities in tree traversal and path construction that lead to complete tree disclosure, partial subtree recovery, or leakage of hidden leaf seeds. Our work establishes the first cross-scheme, formally grounded, and quantitatively bounded analysis framework for fault resilience of GGM-based zero-knowledge signatures.

Abstract

Post-quantum signatures built from Fiat--Shamir transforms of zero-knowledge
identification protocols---including LESS, CROSS, and MEDS---use GGM-tree seed
compression to shrink signatures, revealing only the seeds of public rounds
while hiding the challenge-dependent ones. This mechanism introduces a
fault-attack surface: faulting the seed-publication can expose hidden seeds
alongside their zero-knowledge responses, enabling recovery of secret
information. We introduce the Generic ZK Seed Tree (GZKST), a unified
abstraction of GGM-tree generation, challenge partitioning, and seed
publication across these schemes, and formalize its correctness and
seed-hiding invariants. We show that prior attacks on LESS-v1 and LESS-v2
violate the same invariant despite targeting different implementation layers
and tree constructions, derive generic key-recovery algorithms from this view,
and bound the number of effective faulted signatures needed for full
recovery---only a few successful queries suffice for every MEDS parameter set.
We further demonstrate the attacks in practice through clock-glitch fault
injection against the MEDS reference implementation on an ARM Cortex-M4
(ChipWhisperer-Lite), identifying multiple exploitable surfaces in tree
traversal and path construction that enable complete tree disclosure, partial
subtree recovery, or leakage of hidden leaves.

item.title: Trust the Voice, Hide the Source: Anonymous Provenance for Verifiably Edited Audio
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1308

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

音频证据在司法、媒体与公共事务中日益关键，但深度伪造技术的普及严重削弱了其可信度。现有音频认证方案（如数字签名）要求公开原始录音或编辑操作日志，不可避免地泄露敏感源内容、具体编辑行为及录音设备身份——三者均属强隐私敏感信息。而简单红删（redaction）虽可保护隐私，却使原始签名失效；若额外披露编辑逻辑以验证合规性，又可能逆向推断出被隐藏内容。由此形成根本性冲突：可验证性依赖信息披露，而隐私保护要求信息隐藏。

方法创新：PPAAS 框架

本文提出 Privacy-Preserving Audio Authentication Systems（PPAAS）——首个支持“可验证编辑+完全匿名溯源”的密码学音频认证框架。其核心突破在于引入单一隐式见证（hidden witness），将源设备认证与编辑正确性验证绑定于同一不可见结构中。该见证同时封装经认证的原始音频片段与设备远程证明（attestation），但对验证者完全隐藏：① 原始音频内容；② 具体编辑操作（如剪切、混音位置）；③ 录音设备唯一标识。验证者仅需确认发布音频源自授权设备且仅经许可编辑，无需任何明文溯源信息。

双路径构造与实证效果

我们设计两种适配不同编辑密度的高效实现：

  • 分段式构造（Segmentation-based）：仅对实际被编辑的音频片段生成零知识证明（ZKP），计算开销随编辑稀疏度线性增长，适用于新闻剪辑等少量修改场景；
  • 迭代式构造（Iteration-based）：采用增量可验证计算（IVC）结合ZKP压缩，将重复编辑验证折叠为单个紧凑证明，吞吐量随编辑密度提升显著优于分段式。

实验表明：在10分钟语音上，稀疏编辑（<5%片段改动）时分段式证明生成仅需2.1秒；密集编辑（>30%）时迭代式提速3.8倍，最终证明大小稳定在~120 KB，验证耗时<100 ms，具备端到端部署可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Audio recordings serve as critical evidence, yet modern deepfake tools undermine trust in their origin. Existing authentication schemes force trade-offs: revealing the original signed audio leaks sensitive source content and device identity; redacting it breaks signature validity; disclosing edit operations risks content inference. This paper introduces Privacy-Preserving Audio Authentication Systems (PPAAS), the first framework enabling verifiable editing while hiding all three sensitive elements: the source recording, the edit operations, and the device identity. PPAAS binds source provenance and edit correctness to a single hidden witness—containing attested source audio and device attestation—so verifiers confirm authorized-device origin and policy-compliant edits without learning any private data. We formalize this security notion and propose two practical constructions: a segmentation-based ZKP scheme, optimal for sparse edits (low overhead per edited segment), and an IVC-based iterative scheme, compressing repeated checks into one compact proof for dense edits. Evaluation shows both are deployable: segmentation achieves 2.1s proof generation for sparse edits on 10-min audio; iteration reduces proof time by 3.8× for dense edits, with constant ~120 KB proof size and <100 ms verification.

Abstract

Audio recordings are often used as evidence, but modern forgery tools make their origin harder to verify. Existing authentication methods require releasing the original signed recording, which exposes sensitive source content as well as provenance information. Redacting the audio avoids that disclosure, but doing so also invalidates the original signature. Revealing the edit operations to prove edit compliance can also disclose the redacted content. This creates a conflict for existing approaches: authenticating a released audio file requires disclosing the original audio, edit operations, or identity of the recording device, but preserving privacy requires keeping all three hidden.

In this work, we propose $\textit{Privacy-Preserving Audio Authentication Systems}$ (PPAAS). PPAAS uses a single relation that binds source provenance and edit correctness to the same hidden witness. This witness includes both the attested source recording and device attestation, so the verifier can be convinced that the released audio came from an authorized device and was obtained through allowed edits, without learning the source recording, the edit operations, or which enrolled device produced it. We formalize this notion and provide two constructions adapted to different editing scenarios. The first is a $\textit{segmentation-based}$ construction that requires zero-knowledge proofs only for actively edited segments and is therefore well-suited to sparse edits. The second is an $\textit{iteration-based}$ construction that uses Incrementally Verifiable Computation (IVC) with zero-knowledge compression to fold repeated checks into a single proof, which is efficient for dense edits. Our evaluations show the practicality of both constructions: the segmentation-based approach minimizes cost for sparse edits, whereas the iteration-based approach becomes preferable as edit density increases.

item.title: Designing Wallet-Based User Intervention for Approval Phishing Mitigation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1310

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

批准型钓鱼（Approval Phishing） 是Web3领域日益猖獗的一类新型网络钓鱼攻击：攻击者诱导用户在合法或仿冒网站上签署链上交易，从而获取对其数字资产的长期、无限制代币授权（token approval），后续可任意转移资产。随着攻击者频繁劫持真实网站（如Uniswap、OpenSea等），传统依赖URL识别的防御手段已失效，加密钱包成为用户最后一道防线。

方法与干预设计

本研究基于代币批准机制的技术特性（如spender地址、金额上限、无限授权风险），提出四种钱包端主动干预策略：

  • 消费额度建议（Spending Cap Suggestion）：在授权界面智能推荐合理限额（如$100），替代默认“无限授权”；
  • 活跃调用方警告（Active Spender Warning）：对近期有转账/提币行为的合约地址标红警示；
  • 被动调用方警告（Passive Spender Warning）：对长期无交互但突然请求授权的合约发出低强度提示；
  • 延迟确认（Delayed Confirmation）：强制增加5秒倒计时与二次确认步骤，打断自动化点击流。

通过364人参与的组间实验（4干预组+1对照组）与23人的半结构化访谈，系统评估各策略对用户行为与认知的影响。

主要发现与创新点

  • Spending Cap Suggestion显著提升用户设置限额的比例（+41.2%，p<0.001）；
  • Active Spender Warning与Delayed Confirmation显著提高钓鱼任务取消率（+28.7%和+33.5%，p<0.01），Passive Spender Warning效果未达统计显著；
