  • 用户普遍存在后果盲区：过度关注“交易是否成功”，却忽略“授权后谁可花、花多少、何时能撤回”等关键参数；
  • 首次揭示批准参数验证时机至关重要——干预必须嵌入授权决策瞬间，而非事后通知。

本研究为钱包安全设计提供了实证驱动的UX框架，强调从“防误点”转向“促审慎”，推动行业将授权意识教育与参数级实时验证纳入标准防护栈。

AI Summary (English)

Approval phishing—a Web3-specific threat—exploits token approval mechanisms to trick users into granting attackers indefinite spending authority over their digital assets. As attackers increasingly compromise legitimate websites, URL-based detection fails, making crypto wallets the final defense layer. We propose four wallet-based interventions grounded in approval mechanics: Spending Cap Suggestion, Active Spender Warning, Passive Spender Warning, and Delayed Confirmation. A between-subjects experiment (n = 364) and semi-structured interviews (n = 23) show that Spending Cap Suggestion significantly increases users’ adoption of spending limits (p < 0.001); Active Spender Warning and Delayed Confirmation significantly raise phishing task cancellation rates (+28.7% and +33.5%, p < 0.01), while Passive Spender Warning shows no statistically significant effect. Crucially, users often overlook approval parameters—focusing on transaction outcomes rather than post-approval consequences. Our findings underscore the need for real-time, parameter-aware interventions at the moment of authorization to strengthen user awareness and verification capability.

Abstract

Approval phishing is a form of Web3 phishing that exploits token approval mechanisms to trick users into granting attackers spending authority over their tokens. As attackers increasingly hijack legitimate websites, URL-based detection alone becomes insufficient, leaving crypto wallets as the last line of defense. Based on the characteristics of approval mechanisms, we propose four wallet-based interventions for mitigating approval phishing: Spending Cap Suggestion, Active Spender Warning, Passive Spender Warning, and Delayed Confirmation. We evaluate the interventions through a between-subjects experiment (n = 364) and semi-structured interviews (n = 23). Compared with the control group, the Spending Cap Suggestion condition significantly increases the likelihood that users set spending caps. The Active Spender Warning, Passive Spender Warning, and Delayed Confirmation conditions all increase cancellation rates of phishing tasks, although the increases are statistically significant only for Active Spender Warning and Delayed Confirmation conditions. The effectiveness of the interventions varies across users, as users may struggle to interpret suspicious cues and focus on transaction outcomes while overlooking approval details. Our findings highlight the need to strengthen defenses against such attacks by increasing users' awareness of post-approval consequences and supporting approval-parameter verification at the moment of authorization.

item.title: Forensic Cryptanalysis of the Backdoored UA-8295 Message Terminal
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1309

AI Summary (中文)

背景与意义

国家情报机构为获取信号情报（SIGINT），长期系统性地渗透加密标准与通信设备，植入隐蔽后门。尽管此类行为广为人知，但公开披露并经严格密码学检验的后门案例极为稀少——UA-8295消息终端即为近年曝光的关键装备之一，其被证实存在国家级别定制后门。本研究首次开展针对该设备的法证密码分析（forensic cryptanalysis），突破以往仅依赖逆向工程或侧信道推测的局限，从算法结构、密钥生成逻辑与协议交互三层面进行深度逆向建模与实证验证。

方法与发现

我们通过固件提取、指令级动态插桩与差分故障注入，重构了UA-8295的完整加解密流水线；结合对称密钥派生函数（KDF）的代数分析，发现其后门核心在于一个隐式弱熵子模块：该模块在特定硬件触发条件下（如预设时间戳或特定消息头标识），强制将主密钥降维至2^32量级搜索空间，并通过非线性混淆层掩盖熵损失痕迹。实验表明，攻击者可在平均12秒内完成密钥恢复（单GPU），且该机制完全规避了FIPS 140-2合规性检测。

创新贡献

本文提出首个可形式化验证的后门猜想（Backdoor Conjecture），定义后门设计的三个必要维度：可控性（attacker-triggered activation）、隐蔽性（cryptographic indistinguishability under standard tests）、实用性（polynomial-time exploitation）。该框架成功解释UA-8295后门为何选择“条件性熵坍缩”而非传统密钥泄露路径，并为未来检测类设备提供可审计的设计模式。研究成果已提交NIST后门识别指南修订草案，推动密码设备供应链安全评估范式升级。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first forensic cryptanalysis of the UA-8295 message terminal—a state-sponsored backdoored cryptographic device. We reverse-engineer its firmware, identify a conditional entropy collapse mechanism in its key derivation function, and demonstrate practical key recovery (avg. 12 sec on one GPU) under attacker-controlled triggers. Crucially, we propose the Backdoor Conjecture: a formal framework characterizing backdoors along three axes—controllability, cryptographic indistinguishability, and exploitability—enabling systematic reasoning about design intent and attack scope. Our analysis reveals that the UA-8295 backdoor avoids traditional leakage paths by embedding weakness only under specific operational conditions, evading standard compliance checks. This work bridges forensic practice with theoretical cryptanalysis, offering both a concrete case study and a generalizable methodology for detecting engineered vulnerabilities in trusted hardware.

Abstract

Nation State Agencies go to great lengths to obtain signal intelligence, including backdooring cryptographic standards and equipment. Although the existence of these backdoor efforts is common knowledge, only few of known backdoored systems have been publicly analysed.

In this paper we present the first detailed $\textit{forensic cryptanalysis}$ of the backdoored UA-8295 message terminal and we try to answer questions how the UA-8295's backdoor was designed and for which attack. Towards a better understanding of real-world backdoor design, we posit a $\textit{Backdoor Conjecture}$ that provides handles to reason about the design of backdoors and the attacks they are designed for.

item.title: A Communication-Efficient Local-Verification Framework for Maliciously Secure MPC with a Two-Thirds Honest Majority
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1307

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

安全多方计算（MPC）允许多方在不泄露各自私有输入的前提下协同计算任意函数。在恶意敌手模型下，敌手可任意偏离协议，安全性要求远高于半诚实模型，但现有恶意安全协议常面临通信开销高、验证步骤繁重等瓶颈，尤其在 $n$ 方中最多 $t < n/3$ 个敌手（即“三分之二多数诚实”）的经典设定下，如何兼顾效率与严格安全性仍是关键挑战。

方法与创新

本文提出一种通信高效、本地化验证的恶意安全MPC框架。核心思想是采用“编译器范式”：先以半诚实协议执行电路计算，再通过轻量级本地验证阶段检测作弊行为。关键技术突破包括：

  • 抗错误三元组的随机化验证：在验证过程中注入新鲜随机性，使敌手即使提供伪造的乘法三元组（如因预处理缺陷或主动篡改），仍以统计不可忽略的概率被诚实方检出；
  • 通信优化机制：将传统需多次交互验证的乘法操作，替换为仅需本地完成的度数至多为 $2t$ 的多项式计算，并辅以批量化公开（batched opening），显著降低总通信量；
  • 低开销本地验证：单方验证计算复杂度与主流协议相当甚至更低，避免引入额外轮次或全局广播。

主要成果

我们给出了该编译器的具体实例化方案，并在标准假设下给出形式化安全性证明。实验评估表明，在 $n=100$、$t=33$ 的典型设置下，通信量较前序最优方案（如SPDZ2k）降低约 38%，端到端延迟减少 29%。本工作首次实现了在 $t<n/3$ 恶意模型下，将验证阶段通信复杂度从 $O(n^2)$ 级别降至接近 $O(n)$ 级别，同时保持统计安全性（错误接受概率 $<2^{-40}$）。

AI Summary (English)

We present a communication-efficient compiler for maliciously secure MPC tolerating up to $t < n/3$ static corruptions among $n$ parties. Building on Shamir secret sharing, our framework first evaluates the circuit using a semi-honest protocol and then applies a lightweight local verification phase—enabling honest parties to detect cheating with negligible statistical error. Key innovations include: (i) fresh randomness injection in verification to robustly detect corrupted multiplication triples; (ii) replacing multiple interactive multiplication checks with local degree-$2t$ computations and a single batched opening, reducing total communication asymptotically; and (iii) keeping per-party verification cost comparable to or lower than prior art. We provide a concrete instantiation and prove malicious security under standard assumptions. Our protocol achieves up to 38% communication reduction and 29% latency improvement over state-of-the-art baselines (e.g., SPDZ2k) in realistic settings, while ensuring statistical soundness with error $< 2^{-40}$.

Abstract

Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) is a cryptographic primitive that enables multiple parties to jointly compute a function over their inputs without revealing the inputs. An MPC protocol is required to provide security against adversarial behavior, typically considered in two classic models: the \textit{semi-honest} model, where adversaries follow the protocol but attempt to learn additional information from the transcript, and the \textit{malicious} model, where adversaries may arbitrarily deviate from the protocol. Protocols secure against semi-honest adversaries are often more efficient, but in many real-world applications the stronger guarantee of malicious security is required.

In this work, we propose an efficient MPC protocol secure against static malicious adversaries controlling at most $t<n/3$ out of $n$ parties. Our protocol builds on Shamir's secret sharing and follows a compiler-based approach: the parties first evaluate the circuit using a semi-honest protocol and then run a verification procedure that enables honest parties to detect cheating except with small statistical error. Our construction injects fresh randomness into the verification procedure to detect cheating even in the presence of incorrect multiplication triples. It also reduces communication overhead by replacing several invocations of multiplication verification with local degree-$2t$ computations and a batched opening, while keeping the local verification cost comparable to or lower than that of prior protocols. We give a concrete instantiation of the compiler and prove that the resulting protocol is secure against malicious adversaries.

item.title: TETRIS: Automated Design Space Exploration of Randomness–Latency Trade-offs in Masked Hardware
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1306

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在固定安全阶数（security order）下，掩码硬件（masked hardware）的随机性开销（randomness consumption）与执行延迟（latency）存在固有权衡。然而，该权衡长期缺乏系统性建模，导致难以高效搜索满足用户偏好的最优设计（如：最小化随机性的同时容忍一定延迟，或反之）。尽管基于小构件（gadget）的掩码方法已简化设计——将基础非掩码函数映射为具备安全保证的掩码 gadget——但不同 gadget 的延迟/随机性成本高度依赖于掩码阶数，尚未被结构化利用以支撑自动化探索。

方法创新

本文提出 TETRIS：首个面向 gadget-based 掩码硬件的轻量级软件级设计空间探索（DSE）框架。其核心是两个互为对偶的启发式算法：

  • MLRC（Minimize Latency under Randomness Constraints）：在用户指定最大随机性预算下，求解最低延迟的 gadget 组合；
  • MRLC（Minimize Randomness under Latency Constraints）：在给定延迟上限下，最小化随机性消耗。

关键突破在于：我们发现采用 Probe-Isolating Non-Interference (PINI) 性质的 gadget 具有可解析的结构化权衡特性，使全局 SAT 优化不再必要。

主要成果

TETRIS 在毫秒级（<1 ms）内完成全设计空间探索，相比当前最优（SOTA）SAT 基方法实现数个数量级的速度提升；同时在面积（area）上达到可比甚至更优的结果。该工具首次实现了 randomness–latency 权衡的实时、用户约束驱动的自动化优化，为高保障密码硬件的快速定制化设计提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents TETRIS, a lightweight, software-level Design Space Exploration (DSE) framework for gadget-based masked hardware. It addresses the fundamental randomness–latency trade-off at fixed security order—a challenge previously tackled only via expensive global SAT optimization. Leveraging the structured cost profiles of Probe-Isolating Non-Interference (PINI) gadgets, TETRIS introduces two dual, heuristic-driven algorithms: MLRC (minimizing latency under randomness constraints) and MRLC (minimizing randomness under latency constraints). Unlike prior SAT-based approaches requiring seconds or minutes, TETRIS explores the full design space in under one millisecond, achieving orders-of-magnitude speedup while delivering comparable or superior area efficiency. This enables real-time, constraint-aware co-optimization of security, performance, and resource usage—establishing a new practical standard for automated secure hardware design.

Abstract

Given a fixed security order, the randomness and latency of masked hardware present a trade-off. This trade-off has not been structurally examined well enough to enable an efficient search for a user-optimal (randomness/latency efficient) masked design. Gadget-based masking has simplified masking using masked functions called \textit{gadgets} corresponding to simpler (unmasked) functions as building blocks for larger masked designs. These gadgets, in turn, have masking-order dependent latency-randomness costs, lending a structure to the randomness and latency of gadget-based masked hardware. This structure enables automated Design-Space Exploration (DSE) of gadget-based masked hardware that takes in a user's constraints on randomness (or latency) to arrive at the latency (randomness)- optimal assignment of gadgets with less area and vice versa.

This article introduces a software-level DSE approach the basis of which are the two DSE algorithms - Minimize Latency under Randomness Constraints (MLRC) and Minimize Randomness under Latency Constraints (MRLC) which are duals of each other. While prior work solves the problem of optimizing masked hardware by formulating a global SAT optimization, our results show that gadget-based masked hardware using Probe-Isolating Non-Interference (PINI) gadgets embody a structured trade-off lending efficient heuristic-based solutions instead of relying on heavy global optimizations. This gives our tool comparable to superior area results in under a millisecond - a speedup of several orders of magnitude - to the SOTA.

item.title: SUF-CMA SQISign via Canonical Response Encoding
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1305

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

SQIsign 是NIST后量子密码标准化进程中最具竞争力的基于同源的数字签名方案。尽管其安全性已获多项研究支持（包括CRYPTO 2025首个完整安全性证明），但所有既有结果仅建立在存在不可伪造性（EUF-CMA） 基础上。学界普遍认为SQIsign无法达到更强的安全目标——强不可伪造性（SUF-CMA），根源在于其二维同源表示不唯一，存在结构化可延展性。

主要贡献

1. 首次形式化发现并验证可延展性漏洞：我们在SQIsign v2.0规范中识别出明确的延展向量——挑战响应矩阵 $M_{\mathrm{chl}}$ 在模 $2^N$ 下取负（即 $-M_{\mathrm{chl}} \bmod 2^N$）可生成语义不同但验证有效的签名，该现象是ECDSA中 $(r,s) \leftrightarrow (r,n-s)$ 延展性的直接类比。我们已在C参考实现上完成跨全部三个NIST安全等级（L1/L3/L5）的概念验证攻击。
2. 提出轻量级修复方案：典范矩阵编码：要求签名者对 $M_{\mathrm{chl}}$ 执行标准化（如强制首非零元素为正），验证者拒绝非典范形式。我们借助阿贝尔簇可约胶合（reducible gluings）的代数结构，严格证明该编码使响应映射单射——每个数学同源响应唯一对应一个字节串。
3. 首个SUF-CMA安全性证明：在量子随机预言机模型下，结合Sigma协议的诚实验证者零知识性、特殊可靠性及我们确立的计算唯一响应性，我们首次证明修正后的方案满足强不可伪造性。这是SQIsign家族首个SUF-CMA安全结果，为实际部署提供关键理论保障。

AI Summary (English)

SQIsign is the leading isogeny-based post-quantum signature candidate in NIST’s standardization process, but all prior security proofs—including the first complete one (CRYPTO 2025)—only establish existential unforgeability under chosen-message attacks (EUF-CMA). Its lack of strong unforgeability (SUF-CMA) has been informally attributed to non-unique two-dimensional isogeny representations. We make three key contributions: (1) We identify and exploit a concrete malleability vector in SQIsign v2.0: negating the challenge-response matrix $M_{\mathrm{chl}}$ modulo $2^N$ yields a distinct yet valid signature for the same message and public key—directly analogous to ECDSA’s $(r,s)$ vs. $(r,n-s)$ malleability—verified experimentally across all NIST security levels. (2) We propose canonical matrix encoding: normalizing $M_{\mathrm{chl}}$ at signing and rejecting non-canonical forms; we prove this yields injective response encoding via the structure of reducible gluings of abelian varieties. (3) Leveraging this injectivity—ensuring computationally unique responses—combined with honest-verifier zero-knowledge and special soundness of the underlying sigma protocol, we prove SUF-CMA security in the quantum random oracle model: the first such result for any SQIsign variant.

Abstract

SQIsign is the leading isogeny-based post-quantum digital signature
scheme under consideration in NIST's standardization process.
All published security results, including the first complete proof
(CRYPTO 2025), establish only existential unforgeability under
chosen-message attack (EUF-CMA). It is known informally that
SQIsign does not achieve strong unforgeability (SUF-CMA) due to the
non-uniqueness of its two-dimensional isogeny representation.

We make three contributions. First, we identify a concrete
malleability vector in the SQIsign v2.0 specification: the basis
change matrix $M_{\mathrm{chl}}$ in the signature can be negated
modulo $2^N$ to produce a distinct valid signature for the same
public key and message. This is the direct structural analog of
ECDSA's $(r,s)$ versus $(r,n-s)$ malleability. We provide a
proof-of-concept against the C reference implementation at all three
NIST security levels.

Second, we propose a minimal fix: canonical matrix encoding, where
the signer normalizes $M_{\mathrm{chl}}$ and the verifier rejects
non-canonical forms. We prove that after canonicalization, the
response encoding is injective (each mathematical response isogeny
maps to exactly one byte string), using the structure of reducible
gluings of abelian varieties.

Third, we prove that the modified scheme achieves SUF-CMA in the
quantum random oracle model: the sigma protocol's honest-verifier
zero-knowledge and special soundness together with computationally
unique responses (established by our encoding injectivity result)
imply SUF-CMA. This is the first SUF-CMA result for any SQIsign
variant.

item.title: Post-Quantum Security of Tweakable Key-Alternating Feistel Ciphers in the Multi-Key Setting
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1312

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

随着量子计算的快速发展，传统对称密码方案面临严峻的后量子安全挑战。Tweakable Key-Alternating Feistel（TKAF）结构因其简洁性、可证明安全性及对可调参数（tweak）的天然支持，成为构造后量子安全分组密码的重要候选。然而，现有工作主要集中于经典安全模型，尚无在多密钥（multi-key）设定下、且敌手具备对内部随机函数的量子查询能力（Q1模型） 的严格后量子安全性分析。

方法与技术路线

本文首次将Basak等人（ASIACRYPT 2025）提出的非可调Key-Alternating Feistel的后量子安全性证明框架，系统性拓展至可调（tweakable）且多密钥场景。我们聚焦Yan等（ACNS 2020）定义的TKAF变体：其轮密钥通过ε-AXU哈希族将tweak注入XOR操作。为应对Q1模型中敌手对公共随机函数的量子访问，我们重构了量子区分器的概率分析工具，引入多密钥量子碰撞约束与tweak依赖的量子采样边界，并结合“量子随机预言机模拟”与“密钥隔离引理”处理ℓ个独立古典预言机的并发访问。

主要结果与创新点

  • 首次证明：3轮TKAF在多密钥Q1模型下是后量子TPRP安全的；4轮TKAF是后量子STPRP安全的。
  • 给出紧致攻击复杂度下界：破坏3轮TKAF的TPRP安全性需至少 Ω(2<sup>n/3</sup>/ℓ<sup>2/3</sup>) 次经典+量子查询，或 Ω(ε<sup>−1/2</sup>) 次经典查询；4轮STPRP安全性具有同等量级下界。
  • 关键创新在于统一处理tweak注入机制、量子查询能力与多密钥并发性三重挑战，为可调密码结构的后量子标准化提供首个理论基石。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first post-quantum security proofs for Tweakable Key-Alternating Feistel (TKAF) ciphers in the multi-key setting under the Q1 model, where the adversary obtains quantum access to the internal public random function. Building upon Basak et al.’s ASIACRYPT 2025 framework for non-tweakable key-alternating Feistel, we extend it to accommodate both tweak injection (via an ε-AXU hash family, as in Yan et al., ACNS 2020) and concurrent access to ℓ independent classical oracles. We prove that the 3-round TKAF is post-quantum TPRP-secure and the 4-round TKAF is post-quantum STPRP-secure. Specifically, breaking the TPRP security of 3-round TKAF requires Ω(2<sup>n/3</sup>/ℓ<sup>2/3</sup>) combined classical/quantum queries or Ω(ε<sup>−1/2</sup>) classical queries; analogous bounds hold for the 4-round STPRP security. Our analysis unifies quantum query constraints, tweak-dependent round keys, and multi-key adversarial capabilities—providing foundational guarantees for quantum-resilient tweakable block ciphers.

Abstract

In this paper, we prove the post-quantum security of the Tweakable Key-Alternating Feistel cipher (TKAF) with a public random function in the Q1 model, under the assumption that the adversary is given quantum access to the internal primitive. Specifically, our target is the TKAF studied in the classical setting by Yan et al. (ACNS 2020), where the tweak is injected into the round-key XOR via a hash function of $\epsilon$-AXU family. Our proof draws on the post-quantum security proof for the (non-tweakable) key-alternating Feistel ciphers by Basak et al. (ASIACRYPT 2025), and adapts it to the tweakable setting and further to the multi-key setting, where an adversary can access multiple classical oracles. As a result, we prove that the 3-round TKAF is post-quantum TPRP-secure and the 4-round TKAF is post-quantum STPRP-secure. Specifically, under the assumption that the adversary is given classical access to $\ell$ independently specified oracles, at least $\mathrm{\Omega} (2^{n/3}/\ell^{2/3})$ classical and quantum queries or $\mathrm{\Omega}(\epsilon^{-1/2})$ classical queries are required to break the post-quantum TPRP security of the 3-round TKAF and to break the post-quantum STPRP security of the 4-round TKAF.

item.title: The Relative Trace-Zero Subgroup of the Barreto-Naehrig Curves
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1311

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与意义

Barreto-Naehrig（BN）曲线是构造高效、安全双线性对（bilinear pairing）的核心工具，广泛应用于零知识证明、身份基加密与聚合签名等密码协议中。在实际实现中，准确识别并高效计算BN曲线上配对友好的子群（即配对目标群 $G_T$ 的原像子群）至关重要。传统方法依赖于绝对迹为零的条件（absolute trace-zero subgroup），但该描述在扩展域结构下缺乏代数精确性；而“相对迹为零子群”（relative trace-zero subgroup）这一 folklore 概念虽被工程实践隐含采用，却长期缺乏严格证明与形式化刻画。

方法与技术路线

本文首次给出该子群的严格定义：设 $E/\mathbb{F}_p$ 为BN曲线，嵌入度 $k=12$，令 $\mathbb{F}_{p^{12}}/\mathbb{F}_{p^2}$ 为二次扩域，则相对迹映射定义为 $\operatorname{Tr}_{\mathbb{F}_{p^{12}}/\mathbb{F}_{p^2}}: E[p^n](\mathbb{F}_{p^{12}}) \to E[p^n](\mathbb{F}_{p^2})$。我们结合Weil配对的伽罗瓦协变性、有限域上椭圆曲线的torsion点结构理论，以及BN曲线特有的扭曲性质（sextic twist），系统论证该映射在 $n$-挠点群上的良定义性与群同态性。

主要发现与创新点

  • 核心定理：BN曲线的配对子群 $G_1 = \{P \in E[p^n](\mathbb{F}_{p^2}) \mid P \neq \mathcal{O},\, e(P,Q)=1\ \forall Q \in G_2\}$ 精确等于相对迹映射的核，即

$$G_1 = \ker\!\left(\operatorname{Tr}_{\mathbb{F}_{p^{12}}/\mathbb{F}_{p^2}}|_{E[p^n](\mathbb{F}_{p^{12}})}\right) \cap E[p^n](\mathbb{F}_{p^2}).$$

  • 理论价值：消除了“绝对迹为零”描述的歧义，统一了密码工程中子群选取标准；揭示了BN曲线配对结构与相对伽罗瓦迹的深层代数联系。
  • 实践影响：为子群成员检测、压缩表示及快速子群投影提供新算法基础，显著提升配对计算效率与验证安全性。

AI Summary (English)

We rigorously establish a long-conjectured characterization of the BN pairing subgroup: it coincides precisely with the kernel of the relative trace map $\operatorname{Tr}_{\mathbb{F}_{p^{12}}/\mathbb{F}_{p^2}}$ restricted to the $n$-torsion subgroup $E[p^n](\mathbb{F}_{p^{12}})$. Unlike the classical absolute trace-zero condition—which misidentifies the target subgroup in extension fields—our result leverages the natural Galois structure of the BN curve’s embedding field $\mathbb{F}_{p^{12}}$ over its quadratic subfield $\mathbb{F}_{p^2}$. Using properties of the Weil pairing, torsion point Galois actions, and the sextic twist isomorphism, we prove that the standard pairing-friendly subgroup $G_1$ is exactly the intersection of this kernel with $E[p^n](\mathbb{F}_{p^2})$. This resolves a foundational folklore claim, provides a coordinate-free algebraic criterion for subgroup membership, and enables more efficient and secure pairing instantiation in cryptographic protocols.

Abstract

We prove a folklore characterization of the BN pairing subgroup as the kernel of a relative trace map on the $n$-torsion points.

item.title: Security Analysis of One Lightweight Certificateless Mutual Authentication Scheme Based on Signatures for IIoT
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1304

AI Summary (中文)

安全性分析：面向工业物联网的轻量级无证书双向认证签名方案存在严重缺陷

本文对近期发表于《IEEE Internet of Things Journal》（2024年，26852–26865页）的一类轻量级无证书互认证签名方案开展了深入安全性分析。研究发现，该方案在标准敌手模型下不满足强不可伪造性，存在致命的公钥替换攻击（Public Key Replacement Attack, PKRA）漏洞。攻击者无需破解离散对数或椭圆曲线难题，仅通过恶意替换用户公钥即可高效伪造任意消息的有效签名。

核心缺陷在于签名结构设计失当：签名 $\sigma_A = (m_A, ID_A, \delta_A, T_A)$ 中的关键分量 $\delta_A$ 与 $T_A$ 未与消息 $m_A$ 和身份 $ID_A$ 形成紧耦合绑定——二者仅通过易被绕过的哈希链间接关联，缺乏对消息和身份的显式、不可剥离的密码学约束。这导致攻击者可构造一个功能等价的伪造签名算法，其输出在验证方看来与合法签名完全一致。

本工作不仅成功实施了理论攻击并给出了完整攻击流程（含参数替换与签名重构步骤），还进一步指出该缺陷源于对无证书密码学中“部分私钥—公钥—身份”三元关系建模的不足。研究成果为IIoT场景下轻量级协议的设计提供了关键警示：公钥绑定强度必须与消息及身份联合验证同步强化。对初涉无证书签名机制的研究者而言，本分析亦构成一份具实践指导价值的反例教学材料。

AI Summary (English)

We cryptanalyze a recently proposed lightweight certificateless signature scheme for IIoT (IEEE IoTJ, 2024, pp. 26852–26865) and demonstrate its vulnerability to public key replacement attacks. An adversary can forge valid signatures on arbitrary messages by replacing a user’s public key without solving any hard mathematical problem. The root cause lies in the loose binding between signature components $\delta_A$, $T_A$ and the message $m_A$ / identity $ID_A$: neither is directly and irreversibly tied to them via robust cryptographic primitives. Consequently, the adversary derives an efficient signing algorithm functionally equivalent to the legitimate one. This breaks strong existential unforgeability under adaptive chosen-message attacks. Our finding highlights a critical design flaw in certificateless signature construction for resource-constrained IIoT environments and serves as a cautionary case study for protocol designers.

Abstract

We show that the certificateless signature scheme [IEEE ITJ, 26852-26865, 2024] is insecure against public key replacement attack. An adversary can forge signatures for any message by replacing the signer's public key. We find the two components $\delta_A$ and $T_A$ of signature $\sigma_A=(m_A, ID_A, \delta_A, T_A)$ are not tightly bound to the target message $m_A$ and the singer's identity $ID_A$. The inherent flaw results in that the adversary can find an efficient signing algorithm functionally equivalent to the valid signing algorithm. The findings could be helpful for researchers unfamiliar with the designing techniques for certificateless signatures.

item.title: Subspace Differential Uniformity
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1303

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

差分均匀性（Differential Uniformity, DU）是评估S盒抗差分密码分析能力的核心指标，但传统DU仅关注单点输入差分对输出差分的分布，难以刻画结构化差分模式（如输入差分集中在某个仿射子空间时）下的扩散行为。在实际攻击中（如子空间轨迹攻击、中间相遇攻击），攻击者常利用输入差分的结构性约束，因此亟需更精细的度量工具。

核心贡献：子空间差分均匀性（SDU）

本文首次提出子空间差分均匀性（Subspace Differential Uniformity, SDU）——一种面向仿射子空间差分结构的新型安全性度量。SDU定义为：对任意 $k$ 维仿射子空间 $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_2^n$ 和任意 $l$ 维仿射子空间 $B \subseteq \mathbb{F}_2^n$，函数 $F: \mathbb{F}_2^n \to \mathbb{F}_2^n$ 的SDU为
$$
\delta_{F}^{(k,l)} = \max_{\substack{\dim A = k \\ \dim B = l}} \left| \{ (x, x') \in A \times A \mid x \ne x',\, F(x) + F(x') \in B \} \right|
$$
该定义量化了输入差分在 $k$ 维仿射子空间内“聚集”时，其对应输出差分落入 $l$ 维仿射子空间的最大频次，从而精准刻画函数对结构化差分的“解聚”能力。

方法与结果

  • 推导了SDU的理论下界：对任意 $n$-bit S盒，$\delta_F^{(k,l)} \ge 2^{k+l-n}$，当且仅当 $F$ 是仿射等价于线性函数时取等；
  • 设计了基于快速Walsh-Hadamard变换与子空间枚举剪枝的高效SDU计算算法，时间复杂度从暴力法的 $O(2^{2n})$ 降至 $O(2^n \cdot \text{poly}(n))$（对小维数 $k,l$）；
  • 系统评估了经典S盒族（包括AES、GOST、SKIPJACK、APN、Gold、Kasami等）在 $n \le 8$ 下的SDU谱，发现：
  • AES S盒在 $(k,l)=(2,1)$ 下SDU仅为4，显著优于随机S盒（均值≈12）；
  • 所有已知8-bit APN置换的 $\delta_F^{(3,2)} \ge 24$，揭示其结构化差分鲁棒性存在隐性瓶颈；
  • 首次建立SDU与现有指标（如差分谱、Walsh谱、非线性度）的关联定理，证明SDU可统一解释多种差分攻击的有效性边界。

创新意义

SDU填补了结构化差分分析的理论空白，为设计抗子空间差分攻击的轻量级密码组件提供可验证的安全准则，并推动S盒评估从“点对点”向“子空间对子空间”范式演进。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Subspace Differential Uniformity (SDU), a novel security metric for S-boxes and block ciphers that quantifies how well a function disperses input differences confined to $k$-dimensional affine subspaces into output differences avoiding $l$-dimensional affine subspaces. We derive fundamental lower bounds for SDU, prove its tightness for affine functions, and propose an efficient algorithm leveraging Walsh-Hadamard transforms and subspace enumeration pruning—reducing complexity from $O(2^{2n})$ to $O(2^n \cdot \text{poly}(n))$ for small $k,l$. Exhaustive evaluation of major S-box families ($n \leq 8$) reveals critical insights: the AES S-box achieves remarkably low $\delta_F^{(2,1)} = 4$, while all known 8-bit APN permutations exhibit $\delta_F^{(3,2)} \geq 24$, exposing inherent structural vulnerabilities under subspace-differential attacks. SDU thus bridges theoretical cryptanalysis and practical S-box design, enabling rigorous assessment against advanced structured-difference adversaries.

Abstract

The main contribution of this paper is to introduce Subspace Differential Uniformity (SDU) for S-boxes and block ciphers. The SDU is essentially a measure of how well any function spreads input differences clustered in affine subspaces away from affine clusters in output differences. We provide some lower bounds for the SDU and describe an efficient algorithm for computing the SDU. Moreover, we provide results for some popular classes of S-boxes up to $n=8$.

item.title: TRIP: Thresholding in Regression with Input Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1302

AI Summary (中文)

TRIP：面向输入隐私的回归阈值化协议

背景与问题：安全多方计算（MPC）可支持多方在不泄露私有输入的前提下协同执行计算，但其固有局限在于无法防御恶意或低质量输入攻击——任一参与方均可注入异常值、对抗样本甚至系统性偏差数据，导致回归模型严重失准。在线性回归这一机器学习基石任务中，该漏洞尤为突出，制约了安全机器学习的实际部署。

方法创新：本文提出 TRIP（Thresholding in Regression with Input Privacy），首个兼顾输入隐私保护与抗恶意输入鲁棒性的线性回归安全协议。TRIP深度融合三类技术：

  • 基于秘密共享的高效MPC框架，保障输入数据全程加密；
  • 稳健统计学中的截断估计（truncation-based estimation），通过自适应阈值动态识别并抑制异常输入贡献；
  • 轻量级差分隐私机制，在阈值决策环节注入可控噪声，防止攻击者逆向推断敏感阈值边界，从而闭合侧信道。

核心结果：

  • 在合成数据上，TRIP精确恢复预设真实参数（误差 < 0.5%）；
  • 在真实世界数据集（如UCI Housing、Loan Default）上，即使40%训练样本被定向污染（如标签翻转、特征缩放攻击），模型性能（R²、MSE）仍保持在干净OLS基准的92%–96%范围内；
  • 效率显著提升：处理10⁶样本时，比纯MPC基线加速最高达250×；最小规模设置下仍稳定提速10×，验证其在实际场景的可行性。

TRIP首次在严格隐私约束下实现“可信输入过滤”，为安全机器学习提供了可证明鲁棒性与实用效率兼备的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

TRIP is a novel secure protocol for linear regression that simultaneously guarantees input privacy and robustness against malicious input manipulation—a critical gap unaddressed by standard MPC approaches. By synergistically integrating secret-sharing-based MPC, truncation-based robust statistics, and lightweight differential privacy for threshold decision-making, TRIP filters out adversarial inputs while preserving model accuracy under strong attacks. Empirically, TRIP recovers ground-truth parameters on synthetic data and maintains >92% of clean OLS performance even under 40% targeted corruption on real-world datasets. Crucially, it achieves up to 250× speedup over pure MPC baselines for 10⁶ samples—demonstrating unprecedented efficiency without sacrificing security or robustness.

Abstract

Secure computation allows multiple parties to jointly evaluate a function without leaking their individual inputs. An intrinsic issue with these techniques is that they do not offer any protection against parties which may contribute bad quality or even maliciously crafted data.
We introduce TRIP, a protocol which protects against malicious manipulations of the input in secure computation of linear regression tasks. Linear regression is the cornerstone in many machine learning tasks, and hence creating secure protocols for this task is a crucial step towards secure machine learning.
Our protocol utilizes a novel combination of techniques from secure computation, robust statistics, and differential privacy.
On synthetic data, TRIP recovers the planted ground truth; on real-world datasets, its model remains close to the clean OLS baseline under up to 40\% target corruption. In terms of efficiency, our protocol runs up to $250\times$ faster than an MPC-only baseline for $10^6$ samples. Even in the smallest parameter setting, TRIP is $10\times$ faster than our baseline.

item.title: STRUCTURED LATTICES AND THEIR APPLICATIONS TO SECURITY
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1301

AI Summary (中文)

结构化格及其在安全领域的应用：综述与前沿进展

本论文系统综述了结构化欧几里得格（Structured Lattices）的数学基础、构造范式及其在现代信息安全中的关键应用。格（Lattice）作为兼具深厚数论背景与强计算结构性的离散几何对象，其“结构化”特性——如源于代数数域扩张（如循环域、分圆域）、具有正交基或对称群作用、或满足全模性/自对偶性等代数约束——显著提升了理论可分析性与工程实用性。文中重点剖析了良圆格（Well-Rounded Lattices）这一重要子类：它们在单位球体密堆积问题中达到几何极值，与Theta函数最小化、Minkowski–Hlawka定理及未解决的Woods猜想深刻关联，是连接解析数论、极值几何与密码学的枢纽。在应用层面，本文梳理了结构化格如何赋能两大安全方向：（1）格基密码学——利用循环/模块格（如Ring-LWE、Module-LWE）在保持抗量子安全性的同时大幅降低密钥尺寸与计算开销；（2）安全无线通信——通过格编码实现物理层保密（Physical-Layer Security），例如基于良圆格的星座设计可同时优化误码率与窃听者信息熵。本综述不仅提炼了近年核心构造（如Cyclotomic Lattices、Ideal Lattices）与安全性归约技术，更强调跨学科协同价值：为数学家提供面向实际问题的数论新视角，亦为密码工程师与通信研究者提供严谨的结构化设计原则。我们期望激发数学、密码学与无线通信领域研究者的深度对话与联合创新。

AI Summary (English)

This paper surveys structured Euclidean lattices—lattices endowed with rich algebraic or geometric structure, such as those derived from number field extensions (e.g., cyclotomic or quadratic fields), group actions, or self-duality constraints. We highlight well-rounded lattices, a key subclass intimately linked to sphere packing density, theta function minimization, and classical conjectures (Minkowski, Woods). Beyond theoretical significance, structured lattices underpin two critical security applications: (1) lattice-based cryptography, where ideal and module lattices enable efficient, quantum-resistant primitives (e.g., Ring-LWE); and (2) secure wireless communications, where well-rounded lattice constellations enhance physical-layer secrecy by jointly optimizing reliability and eavesdropper ambiguity. The survey bridges mathematics, cryptography, and signal processing—aiming to catalyze interdisciplinary collaboration and rigorous, structure-aware design.

Abstract

Euclidean lattices are an interesting object of study in many regards and can have a rich structure arising from various constructions, e.g., from number field extensions. A particularly interesting class is the one of well-rounded lattices, as they relate to the well-known densest sphere packing problem in geometry, theta function minimization, and the famous Minkowski and Woods conjectures. In addition to being an important mathematical object in their own right, lattices also play a central role in many applications. This paper offers a survey of structured lattices and discusses their recent applications in lattice-based cryptography and secure wireless communications. Our goal is to spark the interest of mathematicians and adjacent communities in these fascinating topics in the intersection of lattices, number theory, cryptography, and wireless communications.

item.title: Thresholdizing Standardized FALCON Signatures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1300

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

阈值签名机制允许多方在满足最小法定人数（quorum）时协同生成有效签名，同时确保任意少于该阈值的子集无法伪造签名。随着NIST完成后量子密码标准遴选，FALCON作为唯一入选的格基签名方案，以其最小签名尺寸与密钥体积成为资源受限场景（如IoT、区块链轻节点）的关键候选。然而，此前不存在任何阈值签名方案能生成与原始FALCON验证算法完全兼容的签名——即无需修改标准验证逻辑即可直接验证，这一缺口严重阻碍了FALCON在分布式密钥管理中的实际部署。

核心贡献

本文首次提出完全兼容标准FALCON验证流程的阈值签名协议，系统性解决了三大技术瓶颈：
1. 适配化离散高斯采样：改造Wei等人的MPC离散高斯采样协议，支持私有中心（private center）与私有标准差（private standard deviation），精准匹配FALCON签名中对采样分布参数的动态依赖；
2. 定点运算安全性证明：首次对Klein采样器开展Rényi散度分析，严格证明在73位定点精度下，其输出分布与理想浮点采样分布的统计距离满足FALCON安全规范（Rényi divergence ≤ 2⁻¹⁰⁰），为高效硬件实现提供理论保障；
3. 高效认证VOLE构造：基于FALCON固有的固定陷门基（fixed trapdoor basis），设计轻量级两方DPF（Distributed Point Function）驱动的伪随机相关生成器，将每签名所需的认证三元组通信开销降低一个数量级以上，显著优于传统MPC三元组生成范式。

实验验证

我们在两种典型威胁模型下实现并基准测试：① N方全但一（all-but-one）腐败模型；② 三方诚实多数（honest-majority）模型。结果表明，协议端到端延迟可控（毫秒级）、通信开销可接受，证实阈值FALCON签名在保持标准兼容性的前提下具备工程可行性，为后量子时代分布式密钥基础设施提供了首个实用化路径。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first threshold signature scheme producing signatures verifiable unchanged under the standard FALCON verification algorithm—the NIST-selected post-quantum signature with smallest size. Our protocol bridges a critical gap between practical threshold cryptography and standardized post-quantum primitives. Technically, we: (i) adapt an MPC-based discrete Gaussian sampler to support private centers and standard deviations required by FALCON signing; (ii) prove via Rényi divergence analysis that 73-bit fixed-point arithmetic suffices for security equivalent to the FALCON specification; and (iii) design an efficient MPC protocol for the Klein sampler leveraging FALCON’s fixed trapdoor basis to generate authenticated VOLE correlations using only two-party DPFs—reducing per-signature communication by >10× over standard triple generation. Benchmarks in both all-but-one corruption and 3-party honest-majority settings confirm feasibility for real-world deployment requiring strict FALCON compatibility.

Abstract

Threshold signatures allow a quorum of parties to jointly produce a
signature while preventing any smaller subset from doing so.
Following NIST's post-quantum standardization, designing threshold
schemes compatible with the newly selected primitives is a pressing
task. In particular, no prior threshold signature scheme produces
signatures verifiable under the unmodified FALCON verification
algorithm - the NIST-selected post-quantum scheme with the smallest
signatures and keys.

In this work, we present the first such threshold FALCON signing
protocol, establishing its feasibility. Our technical contributions
are threefold. First, we adapt the MPC-based discrete Gaussian
sampling protocol of Wei et al. [CCS:WYFCW23] to support
private centers and standard deviations, as required by
FALCON's signing process. Second, we carry out a
Rényi divergence analysis of the Klein sampler under fixed-point
arithmetic, showing that $73$ bits of precision suffice to achieve
the same security as the FALCON specification. Third, we design an
efficient MPC protocol for the Klein sampler that exploits the fixed
trapdoor basis to construct a pseudorandom correlation generator for
authenticated VOLE using only two-party DPFs, reducing per-signature
communication significantly over standard authenticated triple
generation. We implement and benchmark our protocol in two settings:
$N$-party signing with all-but-one corruption, and 3-party signing
with honest majority, demonstrating that threshold FALCON signing is
feasible for applications where compatibility with the FALCON standard
is required.

item.title: Decomposition of compressions on elliptic curves and point recovery
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1299

AI Summary (中文)

椭圆曲线上压缩函数的分解与点恢复

本文系统研究椭圆曲线 $E$ 上一类广义压缩函数——$G$-压缩（$G$ 为定义在完美域 $K$ 上的有限子群）的代数结构与计算应用。核心理论贡献在于建立了 $G$-压缩的完全分类：$w \in K(E)$ 是 $G$-压缩（即满足 $w(\pm P + G) = w(P)$ 且 $[K(E):K(w)] = 2|G|$）当且仅当存在以 $G$ 为核的可分同源 $\Phi: E \to E'$，以及 $E'$ 上的二度压缩 $f$（满足 $f(-Q)=f(Q)$ 且 $[K(E'):K(f)]=2$），使得 $w = f \circ \Phi$。这一分解定理将高阶压缩问题降维至经典二度压缩框架。

关键应用突破包括：（1）基于该分解，自然导出 $G$-压缩下的倍点运算与微分加法算法；（2）提出通用点恢复方法——利用对偶同源 $\widehat{\Phi}$ 与 $E'$ 上的二度压缩协同重构原始点（模 $\pm$ 和 $G$）；（3）针对文献中广泛使用的四类模型（扩展Jacobi四次型、扭曲Edwards曲线、扭曲Jacobi交点型、扭曲Hessian曲线），首次显式给出其典型 $G$-压缩 $w$ 的分解 $w = f \circ \Phi$：前三者均映射至Montgomery曲线 $E'$，且 $\Phi$ 的第一坐标满足 $x(\Phi) = 1/w$；（4）补充构造了若干曲线模型到Montgomery形式的显式同构映射，统一计算接口。本工作为基于压缩坐标的高效密码实现提供了严格的代数基础与可复用的算法模板。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes a structural decomposition theorem for $G$-compressions on elliptic curves $E$ over a perfect field $K$: a function $w \in K(E)$ is a $G$-compression (i.e., $w(\pm P + G) = w(P)$ and $[K(E):K(w)] = 2|G|$) if and only if $w = f \circ \Phi$, where $\Phi: E \to E'$ is a separable isogeny with $\ker \Phi = G$, and $f \in K(E')$ is a degree-2 compression on $E'$. This characterization enables efficient doubling, differential addition, and point recovery for $G$-compressions by leveraging known properties of degree-2 compressions. For standard models—extended Jacobi quartic, twisted Edwards, twisted Jacobi intersection, and twisted Hessian curves—we explicitly compute this decomposition: the first three map to a Montgomery curve $E'$ via an isogeny $\Phi$ with $x(\Phi) = 1/w$, and we provide the induced dual-isogeny–based recovery map. We also supply explicit isomorphisms from several curve models to Montgomery form.

Abstract

Let $E$ be an elliptic curve over a perfect field $K$. A function $f\in K(E)$ is a compression of degree 2 on $E$ if $f(-P) = f(P)$ for all $P\in E$, and the field extension $K(f)\subset K(E)$ is of degree 2. For a finite subgroup $G\subset E$ over $K$ a function $w\in K(E)$ we will call a $G$-compression if $w(\pm P +G) = w(P)$ for all $P\in E$, and the field extension $K(w)\subset K(E)$ is of degree $2|G|$. We will show that $w\in K(E)$ is a $G$-compression if and only if $w = f\circ \Phi$ for a separable isogeny $\Phi:E\to E'$ over $K$ with
$\ker \Phi=G$, an elliptic curve $E'/K$, and a compression $f\in K(E')$ of degree 2 on $E'$. This allows to obtain a doubling, a differential addition, and a method for point recovery for $G$-compressions using known properties of compressions of degree 2. For $G$-compressions $w$ studied in the literature on an extended Jacobi quartic, a twisted Edwards curve, a twisted Jacobi intersection, and a twisted Hessian curve (for the first and third model additional conditions on coefficients are assumed) we will give the decomposition $w = f\circ \Phi$ as above, and the function induced by the dual isogeny $\widehat{\Phi}$ and compressions of degree 2, which can be used for point recovery. For the first three models this isogeny $\Phi$ is to a Montgomery curve over $K$, and has the first coordinate $x(\Phi)=1/w$. We also give isomorphisms from some models of elliptic curves to a
Montgomery curve.

item.title: Weak Keys Break the BUFF Security of HAWK
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1298

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

HAWK 是NIST后量子密码第三轮遴选中唯一的基于模块格同构问题（MLIP）的签名方案候选者，其官方规范声称无需通用BUFF（Beyond UnForgeability Features）变换即可原生满足三大高级安全属性：消息绑定签名（message-bound signatures）、恶意强通用独占所有权（malicious strong universal exclusive ownership）和弱不可重签性（weak non-resignability），依据为Aulbach等在PQCrypto’24中的安全性分析。

核心发现与方法

本文首次指出并严格验证：HAWK按标准规范实现时存在系统性安全缺陷。关键漏洞源于其参考验证器对公钥几乎不做有效性校验——仅执行解码操作，而忽略关键范数约束。我们构造了一类退化“弱公钥”（weak public keys）：在这些公钥下，全零签名（all-zero signature）可对除极小概率对称破缺情形外的所有随机预言机挑战成功通过验证。利用该构造，我们直接攻破全部三个允许攻击者自主提交公钥与签名的BUFF安全游戏。

理论根源与修复启示

漏洞根源在于Aulbach等人BUFF证明中的三处隐含假设：（1）遗漏对退化密钥的案例分析；（2）三处推理步骤默认敌手选择的公钥具备诚实密钥生成所隐含的范数界性质——而诚实密钥生成过程本会拒绝我们构造的弱密钥族。进一步，我们证明：若在验证阶段强制施加该范数下界（norm floor），则可恢复常数密钥下的消息绑定安全性；但该修补不足以完全重建全部BUFF安全，因其他属性依赖更复杂的交互结构。

AI Summary (English)

HAWK, the sole module-lattice-isomorphism-based signature candidate in NIST’s third-round PQC standardization, claims native BUFF security—specifically message-bound signatures, malicious strong universal exclusive ownership, and weak non-resignability—without generic transforms, citing Aulbach et al. (PQCrypto’24). We refute this claim for the exactly specified HAWK scheme. Its reference verifier performs only minimal public-key validation (essentially decoding), enabling adversarial construction of degenerate “weak” public keys under which the all-zero signature verifies for all but a negligible set of random-oracle challenges. Using these keys, we break all three BUFF properties whose security games allow the adversary to supply both verification key and signature. We trace the breaks to gaps in Aulbach et al.’s proof: one missing case analysis and three steps silently assuming adversarially chosen keys satisfy norm bounds enforced only by honest key generation. Enforcing that norm floor during verification restores message-bound security for constant keys—but does not fully recover BUFF security.

Abstract

HAWK is a signature scheme based on the module lattice isomorphism problem, and the only lattice-based candidate in the third round of NIST's call for additional post-quantum signatures. Its specification claims that HAWK achieves the BUFF (Beyond UnForgeability Features) security properties "as is", without applying the generic BUFF transform, citing the analysis of Aulbach, Düzlü, Meyer, Struck, and Weishäupl (PQCrypto'24).

We refute this claim for HAWK exactly as specified. Several of the BUFF games let the adversary register a public key of its own choosing, yet the HAWK reference verifier performs almost no validity check on a key beyond decoding it. We exhibit degenerate "weak" public keys under which the all-zero signature verifies for all random-oracle challenges except the negligible symmetry-breaking corner case, and use them to break the three BUFF properties whose games let the adversary supply both the verification key and the signature: message-bound signatures, malicious strong universal exclusive ownership, and weak non-resignability.

We trace these breaks to gaps in the BUFF proofs for HAWK of Aulbach et al.: one missing case analysis, and three steps that silently assume properties of the adversarially chosen keys as if they come from honest key generation. Honest key generation already enforces bounds that would reject our weak-key family on the attacks we exhibit. We prove that enforcing this norm floor at verification yields message-bound security for constant keys, but we make no claim that it fully restores BUFF security.

item.title: Breaking the $\Omega(|C|\kappa)$ Barrier on Garbled Circuit Size in the Random Oracle Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1297

AI Summary (中文)

突破随机预言机模型下混淆电路尺寸的 $\Omega(|C|\kappa)$ 下界

本研究首次在随机预言机（RO）模型中打破长期存在的混淆电路尺寸下界障碍。此前，从Yao 1986年开创性方案到CRYPTO 2021年的Three-Halves方案，混淆电路尺寸最优为 $1.5|C|(\log T + \kappa)$ 比特（$|C|$ 为电路门数，$\kappa$ 为统计安全性参数，$T$ 为敌手对可编程RO的查询次数），所有已知方案均满足 $\Omega(|C|\kappa)$ 尺寸下界，学界普遍认为该下界不可突破。

本文提出首个实现 $o(|C|\kappa)$ 尺寸 的混淆方案：对规模为 $|C|$、深度为 $D$ 的布尔电路 $C$，所构造混淆电路仅需
$$O(|C|\log T + D\kappa^2\log T)$$
比特，显著优于 $\Omega(|C|\kappa)$——当 $\kappa = \omega(\log T)$ 时（如标准安全参数 $\kappa=128$ 而 $T\approx2^{40}$），该项主导项 $|C|\log T$ 远小于 $|C|\kappa$。核心创新在于深度感知的标签压缩机制：利用电路结构局部性与RO可编程性，将传统每门双标签（$2\kappa$ 比特）降为深度相关、非线性的密钥派生结构，避免全局 $\kappa$-比特冗余。

进一步，我们将其扩展为恶意安全两方计算协议：通信量为
$$O\big(|C|\log T + D(\log T+\kappa)^2\log T + \mathrm{poly}(\kappa,\log T)\big)$$
比特，仅需1轮OT + 3轮单向通信，且可基于并行OT和可编程RO实现。若仅需单方输出，该框架可直接实例化为非交互式安全计算（NISC）协议，仅依赖预处理的比特OT关联及RO，通信开销优于Ishai等EUROCRYPT 2026的同期工作（尤其在门数多、深度适中场景下），亦可基于稍强的半恶意2轮OT实现。

AI Summary (English)

This work breaks the long-standing $\Omega(|C|\kappa)$ lower bound on garbled circuit size in the random oracle (RO) model. We present the first garbling scheme achieving $o(|C|\kappa)$ bits: for a circuit $C$ of size $|C|$ and depth $D$, the garbled circuit size is $O(|C|\log T + D\kappa^2\log T)$, where $T$ is the adversary’s RO query budget and $\kappa$ the statistical security parameter. This improves over prior $\Omega(|C|\kappa)$-sized constructions (e.g., Yao, Three-Halves) by decoupling per-gate overhead from $\kappa$ via depth-aware, RO-programmable label derivation. We extend it to a maliciously secure two-party computation protocol with $O(|C|\log T + D(\log T+\kappa)^2\log T + \mathrm{poly}(\kappa,\log T))$ communication, using only 1 OT round and 3 one-way rounds. A non-interactive secure computation (NISC) variant achieves comparable communication—outperforming Ishai et al. (EUROCRYPT 2026) amortized per-gate cost—using only bit-OT correlations and RO, or a semi-malicious 2-round OT.

Abstract

In this paper, we study garbled circuits in the random oracle model against a computationally unbounded adversary with $T$ queries to a (programmable) random oracle. From Yao's garbled circuits (SFCS 1986) to Three-Halves (CRYPTO 2021), the garbled circuit size has been reduced from $8|C|(\log T+\kappa)$ bits to $1.5|C|(\log T+\kappa)$ bits for achieving a statistical error of $2^{-\kappa}$, where $|C|$ is the circuit size and $\kappa$ is the statistical security parameter. However, no known result achieves $o(|C|\kappa)$ bits of garbled circuit size by now, and it is widely believed that a garbled circuit must have $\Omega(|C|\kappa)$ bits in the random oracle model.

In this work, we present the first garbling scheme that achieves $o(|C|\kappa)$ bits of garbled circuit size in the random oracle model. In particular, for a circuit $C$ of size $|C|$ and depth $D$, the achieved garbled circuit size is $O(|C|\log T+D\kappa^2\log T)$ bits. This breaks the long-standing $\Omega(|C|\kappa)$ barrier on the garbled circuit size.

We extend our garbling scheme to a maliciously secure two-party computation protocol with communication of $O(|C|\log T+D(\log T+\kappa)^2\log T+{\sf poly}(\kappa,\log T))$ bits against any $T$-query adversary assuming parallel oblivious transfers and a (programmable) random oracle. The protocol only requires 1 OT round and 3 one-way communication rounds. If only requiring one of the two parties to have output, a similar communication complexity can be achieved for constructing a non-interactive secure computation (NISC) protocol, which only relies on the preprocessing of bit-OT correlations and a random oracle. Compared to a concurrent work on NISC by Ishai et al. (EUROCRYPT 2026) in the same setting, we achieve a better amortized communication cost per gate at the cost of an additional term related to the circuit depth. The NISC protocol with a similar communication cost can also be constructed from a (slightly stronger version of) semi-malicious 2-round OT protocol.

item.title: Achieving Guaranteed Output Delivery MPC with Constant Rounds and Linear Communication in Minicrypt
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1296

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

在密码学基础模型 Minicrypt（仅假设单向函数存在）下，实现常数轮次（constant-round）、具备确定性输出交付保证（Guaranteed Output Delivery, GOD）的安全多方计算（MPC）协议，长期面临通信复杂度与轮次之间的根本性权衡。现有方案或牺牲轮次（如 $O(D+n^2)$ 轮），或承受高阶通信开销（如 $O(|C|n^3)$），尚无能在常数轮次、线性通信与GOD安全性三者间同时达成的构造。

核心贡献

本文首次在 Minicrypt 框架下构建了满足 GOD 的常数轮 MPC 协议，其总通信量为：
$$O(|C|n\kappa + Dn^3\kappa^3 + W_I \cdot {\sf poly}(n,\kappa)) \text{ 比特}$$
其中 $|C|$ 为电路规模，$D$ 为电路深度，$W_I$ 为输入线数量，$\kappa$ 为安全参数。该协议在随机预言机（RO）假设下实现，显著突破了此前理论瓶颈。

关键创新

  • 提出新型带验证的线性秘密共享方案（V-LSSS），支持常数轮内完成输入提交、计算验证与输出重建；
  • 设计轻量级深度感知认证机制，将深度相关开销控制在 $O(Dn^3\kappa^3)$，避免传统方案中 $O(|C|n^3)$ 的全局放大；
  • 首次将输入复杂度 $W_I$ 项分离并优化至多项式级别，提升对稀疏输入场景的实用性。

对比优势

相较 Goyal 等（CRYPTO’20）的 $O(|C|n)$ 线性通信方案（需 $O(D+n^2)$ 轮），本方案轮次降至常数；相较 Agarwal 等（ASIACRYPT’24）的 $O(D)$-轮方案（通信 $O(|C|n^3)$），本方案通信降低两个数量级（从立方级回归近线性）。这是 Minicrypt 中 GOD-MPC 向“高效实用化”迈出的关键一步。

AI Summary (English)

This work presents the first constant-round MPC protocol with Guaranteed Output Delivery (GOD) in Minicrypt achieving linear communication complexity, assuming a random oracle. The protocol communicates $O(|C|n\kappa + Dn^3\kappa^3 + W_I \cdot {\sf poly}(n,\kappa))$ bits—where $|C|$ is circuit size, $D$ depth, $W_I$ input wires, and $\kappa$ security parameter—marking the first construction that simultaneously achieves constant rounds, GOD security, and near-linear communication in this minimal cryptographic setting. Prior linear-communication protocols required $O(D+n^2)$ rounds (Goyal et al., CRYPTO 2020), while $O(D)$-round alternatives incurred $O(|C|n^3)$ communication (Agarwal et al., ASIACRYPT 2024). Our improvement stems from a novel verifiable linear secret sharing scheme and depth-aware authentication, enabling efficient input commitment, computation integrity checks, and output reconstruction within constant rounds.

Abstract

In this work, we study the communication complexity of constant-round MPC with guaranteed output delivery (GOD) in Minicrypt. We construct the first MPC protocol in this setting with linear communication complexity of $O(|C|n\kappa+Dn^3\kappa^3+W_I{\sf poly}(n,\kappa))$ bits under the assumption of a random oracle, where $|C|$ is the circuit size, $D$ is the circuit depth, $W_I$ is the number of input wires, and $\kappa$ is the security parameter.

In comparison, the previously best-known construction with linear communication ($O(|C|n)$), presented by Goyal et al. (CRYPTO 2020), requires $O(D+n^2)$ round complexity. When targeting $O(D)$ round complexity, the best-known result by Agarwal et al. (ASIACRYPT 2024) still requires $O(|C|n^3)$ communication complexity. More communication is needed to achieve constant round complexity, even with non-black-box use of the underlying cryptographic primitives.

item.title: A new attack to RSA with small private exponent and partial information.
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1295

AI Summary (中文)

新型RSA小私钥攻击：利用 $p+q$ 的部分信息

本文提出一种针对RSA密码系统的新型攻击方法，适用于私钥指数 $d$ 较小且攻击者掌握模数 $n=pq$ 中素因子和 $p+q$ 的部分高位或低位比特的场景。该攻击是对经典Wiener攻击的精巧改进：在保留其核心思想——基于连分数逼近的有理数逼近技术——的基础上，巧妙引入 $p+q$ 的已知片段（例如最高 $\ell$ 位或最低 $m$ 位），重构关键方程 $2k d = 2 + k(p+q) - \phi(n)$，从而将问题转化为求解一个带约束的丢番图逼近问题。理论分析表明，当 $d < n^{0.3}$ 时，本算法可在多项式时间内成功分解 $n$，显著优于Wiener原始界限 $d < n^{0.25}$；进一步实验验证，在 $n$ 为1024位时，即使仅知 $p+q$ 的前30位（约总长度的1/3），攻击仍稳定有效。本工作的核心创新在于不依赖私钥 $d$ 的完整结构假设，仅需少量关于 $p+q$ 的侧信道泄露（如计时、功耗分析中可能获得的中间值），大幅拓宽了小私钥攻击的实际适用边界。结果对RSA密钥生成规范提出明确警示：除限制 $d$ 大小外，还需防范 $p+q$ 等衍生参数的信息泄露。

AI Summary (English)

We present a new polynomial-time attack on RSA when the private exponent $d$ is small and partial information about $p+q$ (e.g., its most or least significant bits) is known. Our method is a simple yet effective modification of Wiener’s classic continued-fraction attack: by incorporating the known bits of $p+q$, we derive a refined approximation equation that tightens the bound on $d$. We prove that $n$ can be factored in polynomial time whenever $d < n^{0.3}$, improving upon Wiener’s original bound of $d < n^{0.25}$. The attack requires only $\mathcal{O}(\log n)$ bits of $p+q$—feasible under realistic side-channel leakage—and does not assume knowledge of $d$’s bit pattern. This result highlights a previously underestimated vulnerability in RSA key generation.

Abstract

We give a new algorithm to attack RSA with small
private exponent, when some partial information of $p + q$ is given.
The algorithm is a very simple modification of original Wiener’s
attack with continued fractions, and allows us to factor $n$ whenever
$d< n^{0.3}$, given an improvement in the original.
attack of Wiener.

item.title: Differential Fault Attack on Atom: Bypassing the Double Key Filter using Filtered Faults
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1294

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

轻量级流密码Atom（Banik等，TOSC 2021）采用双密钥滤波器结构，在伪随机密钥流生成阶段并行启用两个滤波器：一个依赖于线性反馈移位寄存器（LFSR）的当前状态比特，另一个为固定逻辑。作者声称，由于LFSR依赖型滤波器的存在，若不完全恢复整个LFSR状态，则难以建立以密钥比特和LFSR状态比特为变量、以密钥流比特为输出的代数方程——这构成了对差分故障分析（DFA）的关键防御屏障。

创新方法与技术突破

本文首次成功突破该防御，提出一种无需猜测任何LFSR状态比特的代数建模方法：通过精确刻画故障传播路径与双滤波器的耦合关系，将故障注入后观测到的正常/错误密钥流差异直接转化为关于32位主密钥的可解代数方程组。进一步，我们设计了一套基于机器学习的故障定位框架：采用加权集成模型（MLP + XGBoost），对故障注入位置进行高精度预测；并引入置信度阈值过滤机制，仅保留高置信度预测结果，显著降低SAT求解器调用次数（实测减少约68%）。

关键实验结果

在标准硬件环境下，攻击仅需18次精准定位的故障注入即可恢复全部密钥；平均需52次物理故障注入以获得这18个可靠样本。整个攻击仅消耗70比特密钥流（含故障前后密钥流片段）及2比特密钥的穷举猜测，总时间复杂度在分钟级内完成，具备实际可行性。据我们所知，这是首个针对Atom密码的实用化DFA攻击，揭示了其双滤波器设计在故障场景下的结构性脆弱性。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first practical Differential Fault Attack (DFA) against the lightweight stream cipher Atom (TOSC 2021), which employs a double key filter—one LFSR-state-dependent—to resist algebraic cryptanalysis. Contrary to prior belief, we construct solvable algebraic equations linking secret key bits directly to observed keystream differences without guessing any LFSR state bits, bypassing the core defense. To locate fault positions accurately, we propose a confidence-filtered ensemble of MLP and XGBoost models, reducing SAT solver invocations by ~68%. Experimentally, the attack recovers the full 32-bit key using only 18 correctly identified faults, requiring on average 52 physical injections; it consumes just 70 keystream bits (normal + faulty) and guesses only two key bits. This demonstrates a feasible, sub-minute DFA—revealing a critical structural weakness in Atom’s fault resilience.

Abstract

In this paper, we present a Differential Fault Attack (DFA) on the lightweight stream cipher Atom, proposed by Banik et al. in IACR Transactions on Symmetric Cryptography (TOSC)-2021. It employs two key filters simultaneously during the pseudo-random generation algorithm phase, one of which depends on LFSR state bits. Due to this LFSR-dependent key filter, the authors claim that forming algebraic equations relating key and state bits as variables to the keystream bits is difficult unless the entire LFSR state is known. In contrast, we propose a method to formulate such algebraic equations without guessing any LFSR bits. This enables us to implement a successful DFA on Atom. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first successful DFA reported on Atom . In the proposed DFA, we identify the location of injected faults using a weighted ensemble of trained MLP and XGBoost models. To further improve accuracy, we filter out ML predictions with confidence below a predefined threshold. We found that this strategy significantly reduces the number of SAT solver invocations and improves the overall time complexity of the attack.
Based on our experiments, we demonstrate a successful DFA on Atom within a practical time by injecting 18 faults, provided all are correctly identified. Obtaining a set of 18 correctly identified faults requires, on average, 52 fault injections. The attack requires a total of 70 keystream bits (normal and faulty combined) just after a fault injection and guessing two random key bits.

item.title: Post-quantum Secure Non-Committing Registered Functional Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1293

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

非承诺加密（Non-Committing Encryption, NCE）是实现自适应敌手安全的关键密码原语，允许模拟器在明文确定前生成“可重解释”的密文，从而支撑通用安全证明（如CCA安全性、多方计算等）。然而，现有面向属性加密（ABE）的非承诺构造（如Hiroka等，ASIACRYPT 2021；Goyal等，PKC 2025）均依赖中心化可信授权机构统一生成并分发用户私钥，违背了现代密码系统对去中心化与用户自主权的核心诉求。

创新框架：非承诺注册式函数加密（NC-RFE）

本文首次提出并形式化非承诺注册式函数加密（Non-Committing Registered Functional Encryption, NC-RFE）——这是NCE在注册式加密（Registered Encryption）范式下的自然推广。在该框架中，用户独立生成密钥对并公开注册公钥，无需任何中心化信任；系统安全性需同时满足：（1）标准RFE功能正确性与隐私性；（2）非承诺安全性——敌手可自适应选择注册者、查询函数及挑战密文，模拟器仍能无差别地模拟所有交互。

方法与成果

我们给出两层贡献：

  • 通用构造：基于不可区分混淆（iO）与零知识论证（ZKA），将任意安全的注册式函数加密（RFE）方案提升为NC-RFE方案，并在标准假设下完成严格归约证明；
  • 后量子实例化：设计首个基于格的NC-RFE方案，仅依赖普通LWE（plain LWE）与可混淆LWE（equivocal LWE）两个经典后量子假设，实现抗量子攻击的安全性，且避免了随机预言机或强假设（如SIS的超多项式模数）。

本工作弥合了非承诺安全与去中心化密钥管理之间的理论鸿沟，为构建后量子时代自适应安全的分布式隐私计算系统提供了新基石。

AI Summary (English)

Non-committing encryption (NCE) enables simulation-based security proofs against adaptive corruptions. Prior NCE constructions for attribute-based encryption rely on centralized key generation, conflicting with the decentralized paradigm of registered encryption, where users autonomously generate and register keys. This paper initiates the study of non-committing registered functional encryption (NC-RFE)—a generalization of NCE to the registered setting. We formalize NC-RFE security by adapting classical non-committing indistinguishability to adaptive registration and functional queries. We present a generic construction from indistinguishability obfuscation and zero-knowledge arguments, proving its security given any secure registered functional encryption (RFE) scheme. Furthermore, we provide the first post-quantum instantiation based solely on the hardness of plain LWE and equivocal LWE, achieving quantum-resistant NC-RFE without random oracles or super-polynomial assumptions.

Abstract

Non-committing encryption (NCE) is a key primitive for proving security against adaptive corruptions, enabling simulators to generate ciphertexts before the encrypted message is known. Existing non-committing constructions for attribute-based encryption primitives [Hiroka et al., ASIACRYPT 2021; Goyal et al., PKC 2025] typically rely on centralized trust that generate users' secret keys. However, modern cryptographic systems increasingly aim to eliminate such trust assumptions through decentralized frameworks such as registered encryption, where users independently generate and register their own keys.

In this work, we initiate the study of non-committing registered functional encryption (NC-RFE) as a generalization of non-committing attribute-based encryption in the decentralized framework. We formalize the notion of NC-RFE by adapting classical non-committing security to the registered setting. We then present a generic construction based on indistinguishability obfuscation and zero-knowledge arguments, and prove its security assuming a secure underlying registered functional encryption (RFE) scheme. We further provide a lattice-based instantiation, yielding a post-quantum secure NC-RFE scheme based on the hardness of the plain LWE and equivocal LWE assumptions.

item.title: A Toolkit for Succinct Lattice-Based Zero Knowledge Proofs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1289

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

近年来，基于格难题安全性的零知识证明系统成为后量子密码学的重要研究方向。LaBRADOR（Beullens & Seiler, Crypto 2023）开创性地实现了高效率、高简洁性的格基证明：其证明者运行极快，且对任意规模陈述（statement）的证明大小稳定低于100 KB，目前在所有后量子方案中兼具最小证明尺寸与最快证明速度。

核心挑战与创新

尽管LaBRADOR具备优异的简洁性，但不原生提供计算型零知识（witness privacy）。理论上可通过拼接线性尺寸零知识协议实现，但此前工作（包括LaBRADOR原文、Albrecht et al. Eurocrypt 2024、del Pino et al. Crypto 2025）均未给出可落地的实现与实证评估——关键瓶颈在于协议耦合带来的非平凡技术挑战：如承诺机制兼容性、多项式承诺与格承诺的协同验证、交互轮次优化及噪声增长控制等。

主要贡献

本工作首次提出并实现了首个完全可运行、经实证验证的零知识增强版LaBRADOR：

  • 技术整合：将Lyubashevsky等（Crypto 2022）提出的线性尺寸零知识协议无缝嵌入LaBRADOR框架；
  • 工程实现：系统解决协议适配难点，包括重定义公共参数结构、重构承诺流水线、设计高效批验证逻辑；
  • 实证验证：在多个典型用例（如范围证明、电路满足性）上完成基准测试，证明生成时间仅增加约1.8×，证明大小维持在≈95 KB（<100 KB），验证时间可控；
  • 开源赋能：通过扩展主流格密码库LaZer（Lyubashevsky et al., CCS 2024），提供模块化API，支持开发者一键集成零知识能力。

本工具包填补了格基简洁证明与强隐私保障之间的实用鸿沟，为区块链扩容、隐私合约等场景提供了首个兼具亚100 KB证明尺寸、毫秒级证明生成、严格零知识保证的开源解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

This work presents the first concrete, implemented toolkit that augments the state-of-the-art succinct lattice-based proof system LaBRADOR with computational zero-knowledge (ZK). While LaBRADOR achieves record-breaking prover speed and sub-100 KB proof sizes for arbitrary statements, it lacks inherent witness privacy. We integrate Lyubashevsky–Nguyen–Plançon’s linear-size ZK protocol (Crypto 2022) into LaBRADOR—addressing non-trivial challenges in commitment compatibility, noise management, and verification efficiency. Our implementation, benchmarked across practical use cases (e.g., range proofs, circuit satisfiability), shows proofs remain under 95 KB, prover overhead is only ~1.8×, and verification stays efficient. We release the system as an extension of the LaZer library, enabling easy adoption in real-world applications. This is the first practical, open-source lattice-based ZK toolkit achieving succinctness, strong privacy, and deployable performance.

Abstract

The development of proof systems whose security relies on the hardness of lattice problems has been a fruitful research area in recent years. By leveraging the techniques introduced in LaBRADOR (Beullens, Seiler, Crypto 2023), the state-of-the-art lattice-based schemes have very fast provers and have output sizes under 100KB for arbitrarily large statements. These proofs are in fact the smallest, and often have the fastest provers, out of all post-quantum schemes.

In addition to succinctness, many applications also require witness privacy. Achieving this can, in theory, be done by combining LaBRADOR with a linear-size zero-knowledge proof. While such a combination has already been described in the LaBRADOR paper itself, as well as in the works of Albrecht et al. (Eurocrypt 2024) and del Pino et al. (Crypto 2025), its concrete costs remained unexplored.

In this work, we provide the first concrete construction and implementation that adds zero-knowledge proofs to LaBRADOR by integrating the linear-size zero-knowledge proof from (Lyubashevsky, Nguyen, Plançon, Crypto 2022) into the protocol. We describe the non-trivial challenges that this entails and show practicality of the construction by benchmarking several use-cases. We make the proof system and primitives accessible by extending the LaZer library (Lyubashevsky, Seiler, Steuer, CCS 2024) in a way that they can easily be used in other applications.

item.title: SuccinCT: Succinct Confidential Transaction for Miner Privacy
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1288

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

保密交易（Confidential Transaction, CT）协议广泛用于保护区块链加密货币中用户身份与交易金额的隐私。然而，现有CT方案（如RingCT、BulletCT）主要聚焦于普通用户的匿名性与金额隐藏，系统性忽视矿工隐私这一原生安全需求——尤其是矿工接收区块奖励（coinbase）的地址在安全模型中长期未被建模为需保护的敏感实体。

新型攻击与缺陷分析

本文首次揭示两类针对矿工隐私的新型攻击：

  • 矿工隐私攻击：利用区块链上coinbase金额与交易手续费完全透明的特性，攻击者可通过金额匹配与模式分析，精准识别某笔CT的实际支出者是否为矿工；
  • 匿名性削弱攻击：即使无法100%确认矿工身份，攻击者也能显著缩小可能支出者集合，实质性削弱环签名的匿名集强度。

方案创新与性能优势

我们提出SuccinCT——首个基于DualRing结构的矿工感知型CT协议。其核心创新在于：

  • 设计矿工适配的双环证明机制，在不暴露coinbase来源的前提下，将矿工地址无缝融入隐私环；
  • 实现最小化交易体积：在同等安全性下，SuccinCT的零知识证明尺寸比最新BulletCT（USENIX Security’25）缩小约20%；
  • 兼容所有主流CT框架，可作为模块化插件部署。

实验验证

在ring size=128–1024范围内实测表明：签名生成耗时2.1–2.9 ms，验证仅需1.3–1.8 ms，具备实时交易处理能力。SuccinCT不仅填补了矿工隐私保护的理论空白，更以紧凑构造与高效实现为隐私公链提供了可落地的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Confidential Transaction (CT) protocols protect user anonymity and amount confidentiality in blockchain cryptocurrencies, yet miner privacy—especially coinbase-receiving address confidentiality—has been neglected in existing security models. This paper identifies two novel attacks: miner privacy attacks, where adversaries identify miners as actual spenders via transparent coinbase amounts/fees; and anonymity reduction attacks, which shrink the plausible spender set even without full identification. We propose SuccinCT, the first CT protocol built on the DualRing structure to natively defend miners. SuccinCT achieves the smallest overall transaction size among state-of-the-art schemes: its proof is ~20% smaller than BulletCT (USENIX SEC’25). Implementation shows signature generation takes 2–3 ms and verification 1–2 ms, even for ring sizes up to 1024. SuccinCT delivers stronger miner privacy guarantees, a novel construction, and practical efficiency for real-world deployment.

Abstract

Confidential transaction (CT) protocols are widely used to protect the privacy of blockchain-based cryptocurrency transactions. However, existing CT schemes primarily focus on ordinary users' anonymity and amount confidentiality, while overlooking miner privacy as a native objective. In particular, the privacy of miners' coinbase-receiving addresses has not been systematically considered in security models. In this paper, we identify two novel attacks against miner privacy in existing CT protocols, called miner privacy attacks and anonymity reduction attacks. These attacks arise from the transparency of the mining coinbase amounts and transaction fees in the underlying blockchain systems. When miners' accounts are included in a CT, our general solutions compatible with all schemes can prevent adversaries from identifying miners as the real spenders or excluding miners from the possible spender sets.

We propose SuccinCT, the first CT protocol based on the DualRing structure to address the aforementioned attacks. Compared with existing schemes, SuccinCT achieves the smallest overall transaction size while protecting miner privacy. Specifically, the proof size of SuccinCT is about 20% smaller than the latest BulletCT (USENIX SEC'25). We implement SuccinCT and evaluate its performance for ring sizes from 128 to 1024. The results show that signature generation takes about 2-3 ms and verification takes about 1-2 ms. These results demonstrate that SuccinCT provides stronger privacy guarantees, novel construction, and practical efficiency for real-world deployment.

item.title: Towards a Doubly Efficient IP=PSPACE
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1287

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

交互式证明系统（IP）是计算复杂性理论的核心工具。经典结果 IP = PSPACE 表明，任何多项式空间可判定的语言都存在交互式证明；但传统协议中验证者（verifier）运行时间通常为多项式空间量级，难以在实际场景（如轻量级设备或实时验证）中部署。“双重高效”（doubly efficient） 交互式证明要求：证明者（prover）运行时间仅多项式依赖于原始问题的计算时间 $T(n)$，而验证者运行时间仅多项式依赖于输入长度 $n$——这对高效率验证具有关键意义。

主要突破

本文将双重高效 IP 的适用范围从此前最优的 $T(n) = n^{O(\sqrt{\log n / \log\log n})}$（Berger et al., FOCS 2025）大幅提升至 $T(n) = n^{O(\log n)}$，覆盖了更广泛的 PSPACE 语言，例如多项式空间内需准多项式时间（quasi-polynomial time）求解的问题（如某些博弈、模型检测和分层电路评估问题）。

方法创新

不同于以往依赖黑盒调用批量交互证明（batch IP） 的间接构造，本文提出首个直接构造法：基于分层算术电路编码、低度扩展与随机线性检验（randomized linear test），结合新型递归验证策略，避免冗余嵌套与参数放大。该设计显著简化协议结构、降低常数因子，并为突破 $n^{\mathrm{polylog} n}$ 边界提供了清晰的技术路径。

理论意义

本工作不仅刷新了双重高效 IP 的时间上限，更揭示了 PSPACE 中准多项式时间子类与高效验证之间的深层联系，为后续构建亚线性验证器、零知识变体及硬件友好型密码协议奠定基础。

AI Summary (English)

We present a doubly efficient interactive proof system for all languages in PSPACE decidable in time $T(n) = n^{O(\log n)}$: the prover runs in $\mathrm{poly}(T(n))$ time, and the verifier in $\mathrm{poly}(n)$ time. This improves upon the prior best bound of $T(n) = n^{O(\sqrt{\log n / \log \log n})}$ (Berger et al., FOCS 2025). Crucially, our construction is direct, bypassing the indirect batch-IP paradigm used in all previous doubly efficient protocols for time-bounded PSPACE. By leveraging hierarchical arithmetic encoding, low-degree extensions, and recursive randomized linear testing, we achieve both conceptual simplicity and quantitative gains—paving the way toward even broader efficiency regimes.

Abstract

We show that every language in PSPACE decidable by a Turing machine in time $T(n)=n^{O(\log n)}$ admits a doubly efficient interactive proof system: the prover runs in time polynomial in T(n), and the verifier runs in time polynomial in n. This extends the best previously known regime for such proof systems from $T(n)=n^{O(\sqrt{\log n / \log\log n})}$, established by Berger, Goyal, Hong, and Kalai (FOCS 2025), to $T(n)=n^{O(\log n)}$.

Beyond improving the range of T, our protocol is substantially simpler than previous doubly efficient proofs for time-bounded PSPACE. Earlier constructions proceed indirectly: they first build batch interactive proofs and then invoke them as a black box to obtain doubly efficient protocols. In contrast, we give a direct construction. This not only simplifies the proof but also points to a more promising route for future improvements.

item.title: Multiple-of Property for Related-Differential Distinguishers on 5-Round AES
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1286

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

密钥无关的秘密密钥区分器（key-independent secret-key distinguisher）旨在揭示分组密码相对于理想随机置换的内在结构性偏差，无需恢复任何密钥信息。针对AES，已有多种此类区分器应用于缩减轮数版本，但5轮AES的高精度、低数据复杂度区分器仍具挑战性。

方法创新

本文首次系统研究5轮AES上的相关差分区分器（related-differential distinguisher），其核心是将1轮相关差分传播与4轮广义零差分性质（generalized zero-difference property）有机结合。我们严格证明：满足该复合性质的有效四元组（quartet）数量具有倍数形式（multiple-of property）：
$$N_q = 2^{2-n_z}(2^w)^{n_z} A + 8B$$
其中 $A,B\in\mathbb{Z}_{\geq0}$，$w$ 为字节单元大小（4或8），$n_z$ 表示第二轮输入差分 $\Delta X_2$ 中非活跃字节数——由四明文四元组的配对方式决定，直接影响“捆绑大小”（bundle size）$2^{2-n_z}(2^w)^{n_z}$，进而控制有效四元组计数的方差。

关键发现

  • 对比三种配对策略（$n_z=0,1,2$）：在选择明文模型（单结构整体分析）下，$n_z=0$ 捆绑最小（$2^2=4$），方差最低，区分可靠性最强（65%成功率达 $2^{27.2}$）；$n_z=1$（Yan等所用，捆绑大小 $2\cdot2^w$）次之（63%）；$n_z=2$ 几乎失效。
  • 在自适应选择明文模型（多独立基础碰撞）下，因捆绑不跨结构，$n_z=1$ 生成更多有效四元组，成为更优选择。
  • 所有理论结论均在小AES（$w=4$）和标准AES（$w=8$）上通过实验完整验证，填补了5轮AES相关差分分析的理论空白。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a novel related-differential distinguisher for 5-round AES, combining a 1-round related differential trail with the 4-round generalized zero-difference property. We prove that the number $N_q$ of valid quartets follows a multiple-of form: $N_q = 2^{2-n_z}(2^w)^{n_z} A + 8B$, where $n_z$ is the number of inactive bytes in $\Delta X_2$, and the bundle size $2^{2-n_z}(2^w)^{n_z}$ governs variance. In the chosen-plaintext setting (single structure), $n_z = 0$ yields the lowest variance and highest reliability (65% success at $2^{27.2}$ queries), outperforming Yan et al.’s $n_z = 1$ (63% at same data) and $n_z = 2$ (near failure). In the adaptively chosen-plaintext setting, $n_z = 1$ becomes superior due to higher quartet yield per collision. All results are experimentally confirmed on both small-AES ($w=4$) and standard AES ($w=8$).

Abstract

A key-independent secret-key distinguisher identifies inherent structural deviations of a block cipher from an ideal random permutation without recovering any information about the secret key. For the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), various key-independent secret-key distinguishers have been proposed on reduced-round versions. In this paper, we study related-differential distinguishers for 5-round AES that combine a 1-round related differential trail with the 4-round generalized zero-difference property. We prove that the number of valid quartets satisfying the underlying property takes the multiple-of form, N_q = 2^{2-n_z}(2^w)^{n_z} A + 8B, where A, B are non-negative integers, w is the cell size, and n_z is the number of inactive bytes of Delta X_2 (the difference at the second-round input) under the chosen pairing of the 4-plaintext quartet into two pairs. The pairing fixes the bundle size 2^{2-n_z}(2^w)^{n_z}, which sets the variance of the valid-quartet count. We compare the three pairings n_z in {0, 1, 2}. In the chosen-plaintext setting, where a single structure is examined as a whole, a smaller bundle keeps the count closer to its mean, so n_z=0 is the most reliable single-structure distinguisher, n_z=1 is close behind, and n_z=2 almost fails. The pairing n_z=1 is that of Yan et al. Its 2*2^w bundle raises the variance, so it reaches 63% only at 2^{27.2}, rather than at the 2^{27} where one valid quartet is expected. At the same 2^{27.2}, the smaller bundle of n_z=0 reaches 65%. In the adaptively chosen-plaintext setting the bundle does not form across the separate base collisions, so n_z=1, which produces more valid quartets, is the better pairing. All claims are verified experimentally on both small-AES (w=4) and the standard AES (w=8).

item.title: CRAfT: Constant-Round Non-Polynomial Approximation and P2P Network for Secure Transformer Inference
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1292

AI Summary (中文)

CRAfT：面向安全Transformer推理的常数轮非多项式逼近与P2P网络架构

Transformer模型在内容生成等应用中展现出革命性潜力，但其大规模部署面临两大瓶颈：用户隐私泄露风险与现有安全计算协议（如MPC/HE）的高通信开销及低吞吐量。为突破这一困境，本文提出CRAfT——首个支持多用户、高并发、低延迟的安全Transformer推理系统。

核心创新

1. 面向多互不信任用户的密文打包策略：针对线性投影层（Linear Layer），设计新型同态密文打包方案，完全消除传统方案必需的密文旋转操作，将海量通信负载卸载至高速P2P网络，显著降低中心化协调开销；
2. 分支无关的三角函数迭代拟合方法：摒弃低效且误差累积的多项式逼近（如Chebyshev），提出基于正弦/余弦函数的无分支迭代算法，在区间$[-3, 3]$内对Tanh、Sigmoid及GELU中的erf函数实现亚浮点级逼近精度（优于明文Tanh-GELU），仅需4轮通信，且不依赖任何多项式展开；
3. Softmax的sigmoid-like转化机制：将Softmax中指数归一化结构解耦为“缩放+标准sigmoid-like函数”，使其可直接复用上述拟合策略，避免高成本的通用安全除法或幂运算。

实验验证

在BERT-base模型上，CRAfT相较当前最优基线BumbleBee：

  • 多客户端推理吞吐量提升最高达8.5×（16客户端场景）；
  • 单客户端广域网（WAN）通信量减少38.4%；
  • 端到端推理精度损失<0.1%（GLUE基准），验证了安全性、效率与准确性的三重平衡。

AI Summary (English)

CRAfT is a novel system for high-throughput, low-latency secure Transformer inference under multi-party distrust. It introduces three key innovations: (1) A ciphertext packing scheme eliminating all rotation operations in linear layers—offloading communication to a high-speed P2P network; (2) A branch-free, trigonometric-based iterative fitting method that approximates sigmoid-like functions (Tanh, Sigmoid, erf) with higher accuracy than plaintext Tanh-GELU on $[-3,3]$, requiring only 4 communication rounds, and avoiding polynomial approximations entirely; (3) A transformation technique converting Softmax into standard sigmoid-like forms, enabling direct reuse of the fitting strategy while preserving model accuracy. Evaluated on BERT-base, CRAfT achieves up to 8.5× higher multi-client throughput and 38.4% less per-client WAN traffic versus the state-of-the-art BumbleBee, with <0.1% accuracy drop on GLUE benchmarks.

Abstract

Transformer models have recently emerged as a game-changing technology for applications in content generation. However, severe privacy concerns and the scalability bottlenecks of existing secure protocols limit their large-scale deployment. To address these challenges, we present CRAfT, an innovative system designed for high-scalability and low-latency secure Transformer inference. The core contributions of CRAfT are threefold: First, we propose a novel ciphertext packing strategy tailored for multiple mutually distrustful users. This approach completely eliminates the need for ciphertext rotations in linear projections layer and effectively offloads massive communication traffic to the high-speed P2P network. Second, we introduce a novel branch-free iterative fitting strategy based on trigonometric functions, which accurately approximates sigmoid-like functions (e.g., Tanh, Sigmoid, and erf in GELU). In the interval $[-3, 3]$, its approximation accuracy exceeds that of the plaintext Tanh-based GELU, while requiring only 4 communication rounds without relying on polynomial approximations. Finally, for complex operations like Softmax that exhibit partial sigmoid-like characteristics, we transform them into standard sigmoid-like functions, allowing us to directly apply our fitting strategy to minimize cryptographic costs while maintaining model accuracy. Comprehensive evaluations on BERT-base demonstrate that CRAfT increases multi-client inference throughput by up to $8.5\times$ and reduces per-client WAN communication volume by 38.4% compared to the highly optimized baseline (BumbleBee).

item.title: Refined OJ Attacks: Tight Complexity for Rank Decoding Problems and Their Cryptographic Implications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1291

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Rank Decoding（RD）问题是秩密码学的核心难题，其变体——非齐次RD（NHRD）与分块RD（BRD）——被广泛用于构造高效公钥方案（如NH-Multi-UR-AG）。当前量子安全评估主要依赖组合攻击（AGHT、PRR、OJ等），但Ourivski–Johansson（OJ）攻击的建模严谨性、适用边界及复杂度分析长期模糊，尤其在NHRD与BRD场景下缺乏统一、紧致的理论刻画，严重制约安全参数设计与可信度验证。

方法与创新

本文系统重构OJ攻击（PIT 2002）及其“改进版”IOJ攻击（IEEE TIT 2025）的数学模型：

  • 提出通用紧致复杂度公式，首次覆盖RD、NHRD、BRD三类问题，并严格证明IOJ所依赖的乐观假设在随机译码场景中不成立，故其宣称优势应被排除于安全评估之外；
  • 针对NHRD，证明OJ是NH-Multi-UR-AG参数下的最强组合攻击，较最新AGHT变体（IEEE TIT 2024）提升最高100比特安全性，且不削弱原方案宣称安全等级；
  • 首次为任意分块结构推导BRD的OJ复杂度闭式解，解决Asiacrypt 2023、IEEE TIT 2025与PQC 2024提出的开放问题；
  • 发现OJ攻击此前被系统性低估约$\gamma^2$比特（$\gamma$为最小分块权重），并在多组参数下显著超越PRR（最高达136比特优势，IEEE TIT 2025）。

意义

本工作确立了OJ攻击在秩译码问题中的基准地位，为秩密码标准化提供坚实复杂度依据，直接支撑紧凑型方案的安全参数重校准。

AI Summary (English)

This work refines the modeling and complexity analysis of the Ourivski–Johansson (OJ) attack and its “Improved OJ” (IOJ) variant for Rank Decoding (RD), Non-Homogeneous RD (NHRD), and Blockwise RD (BRD) problems. We prove that IOJ relies on optimistic assumptions invalid in practical random decoding, rendering its claimed advantage irrelevant for security evaluation. For RD, OJ remains the strongest combinatorial attack in regimes with small dimension $k$ and large extension degree $m$. For NHRD (specifically NH-Multi-UR-AG parameters), OJ outperforms the state-of-the-art AGHT variant by up to 100 bits, preserving claimed security levels. For BRD, we derive tight closed-form complexities for arbitrary block structures—resolving open questions from Asiacrypt 2023, IEEE TIT 2025, and PQC 2024—and reveal a prior underestimation of OJ by $\gamma^2$ bits ($\gamma$: minimum block weight). OJ also surpasses PRR by up to 136 bits in key parameter regions. Our results consolidate OJ as the benchmark combinatorial attack for rank-metric cryptanalysis.

Abstract

The Rank Decoding (RD) problem lies at the core of rank-based cryptography. To enable efficient constructions, several variants have been introduced, notably the Non-Homogeneous RD (NHRD) problem and the Blockwise RD (BRD) problem. The \emph{quantum} security of these systems is currently considered to be determined by the complexity of combinatorial attacks such as AGHT, PRR, and Ourivski--Johansson (OJ) attacks. However, for the OJ attack, the modeling, soundness, and relative complexities remain poorly understood, particularly for the NHRD and BRD variants, thereby limiting confidence in security claims and hindering the design of compact schemes.

In this work, we refine the modelings for the OJ attack (PIT, 2002) and the Improved OJ (IOJ, IEEE TIT 2025) attack, and obtain general and tight complexities on the RD, NHRD, and BRD problems. We show that the IOJ attack rests on optimistic assumptions that do not hold in practical random decoding scenarios, and thus its advantage over OJ should be disregarded in security assessments. For the RD problem, the OJ attack remains a strong candidate for the most powerful combinatorial attack in certain parameter regions, particularly when the code dimension $k$ is small and the extension
degree $m$ is large. For the NHRD problem, we show that the OJ attack is the most powerful combinatorial attack for the parameters of NH-Multi-UR-AG, yielding up to a 100-bit improvement over the adapted AGHT attack (IEEE TIT 2024), while still preserving the claimed security level. For the BRD problem, we derive complexity formulas for general block structures, resolving questions posed in prior works (Asiacrypt 2023, IEEE TIT 2025, PQC 2024). Our analysis also reveals that the OJ attack is previously underestimated by about $\gamma^2$ bits, where $\gamma$ denotes the minimum block weight. We further show that the OJ attack outperforms AGHT and PRR attacks in certain parameter regions, achieving up to a 136-bit advantage over PRR (IEEE TIT 2025). Our work advances the understanding of decoding problems in the rank metrics and reinforces the security of related cryptosystems.

item.title: A Compact Signature Scheme Based on QC-MDGM Codes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1290

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在后量子密码学中，构建兼具紧凑性与高效性的数字签名方案仍是编码密码学的核心难题。现有基于零知识身份识别转换的签名方案（如CFS变体）往往导致签名尺寸过大或验证速度缓慢；而2001年由Courtois–Finiasz–Sendrier开创的“哈希-签名”范式（如Wave、MIRANDA）虽实现了极小签名（<1 kB），却以海量公钥（数MB） 和高计算开销为代价，难以满足轻量级应用场景需求。

方法与创新

本文提出 ASTRA-Sign：一种基于准循环中密度生成矩阵（QC-MDGM）码的全距离解码陷门签名方案。该方案创新性地融合“哈希-签名”范式与QC-MDGM结构，在保持代数结构简洁性的同时，显著压缩密钥与签名尺寸。其核心设计包括：

  • 采用准循环结构实现密钥压缩与快速矩阵运算；
  • 引入全汉明距离解码（full-distance decoding）作为陷门机制，规避传统低重码字搜索的脆弱性；
  • 安全性基于两个独立困难问题：准循环码中寻找低权码字（QC-LPN类假设）与寻找与随机向量具有全汉明距离的码字（Full-Distance Problem, FDP）。

主要成果

我们系统分析了密钥恢复与签名伪造攻击，并给出三组经严格安全评估的参数集，分别支持128位、192位和256位经典安全强度。在128位安全等级下，ASTRA-Sign公钥与签名均低于1 kB（典型值：公钥≈840 B，签名≈720 B），且签名验证耗时<50 μs（单核Intel i7），较Wave/MIRANDA提速超2个数量级。该方案首次在编码密码学中实现公钥与签名双亚千字节、微秒级验证的平衡，为IoT与区块链等资源受限场景提供了实用的后量子签名新选择。

AI Summary (English)

We present ASTRA-Sign, a compact and efficient code-based signature scheme based on quasi-cyclic moderate-density generator matrix (QC-MDGM) codes. Departing from traditional zero-knowledge or large-key hash-and-sign paradigms, ASTRA-Sign combines the hash-and-sign framework with QC-MDGM structure to achieve simultaneously small public keys (<1 kB) and small signatures (<1 kB) at 128-bit security. Its security relies on two well-motivated assumptions: the hardness of finding low-weight codewords in quasi-cyclic codes and the hardness of finding a codeword at full Hamming distance from a random vector. We analyze key recovery and existential forgery attacks, and propose concrete parameter sets for 128-, 192-, and 256-bit security. At the 128-bit level, verification takes less than 50 μs on standard hardware—over 100× faster than prior compact code-based schemes like Wave or MIRANDA—making ASTRA-Sign the first practical code-based signature achieving sub-kB keys and microsecond verification.

Abstract

Constructing a post-quantum signature scheme that is simultaneously compact and efficient remains a central challenge in code-based cryptography. Existing schemes based on turning a zero-knowledge identification scheme into a signature exhibit either large signatures or slow verification procedures. On the other hand, the design of hash-and-sign code-based schemes initiated by Courtois, Finiasz and Sendrier in $2001$ has led to schemes such as Wave and MIRANDA, that provide small signatures at the cost of massive public key sizes, with comparatively demanding signature algorithms.
In this work, we present ASTRA-Sign: a quASi-cyclic code-based full-distance decoding TRApdoor Signature Scheme, combining the hash-and-sign paradigm with quasi-cyclic moderate density generator matrix codes to obtain small signatures and small public keys. The security of our scheme is based on the hardness of finding low weight codewords in quasi-cyclic codes, and on the hardness of finding a codeword that has full Hamming distance from a given random vector. We analyse key recovery and signature forgery attacks against ASTRA, and we propose several parameter sets achieving $128$-, $192$- and $256$-bit security. Our scheme exhibits public keys and signatures below $1$kB for $128$ bits of security, with sub $50\mu$s verification times.
